data.output:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# build-fixture-repo.sh — create a clean ddx-initialized git repo for tests / demos.
#
# Usage:
#   scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh <dest> [--profile minimal|standard|multi-project|federated]
#
# Env:
#   DDX_BIN   path to the ddx binary used for seeding (default: ddx from PATH)
#
# Cleanup is the caller's responsibility (rm -rf <dest>).

set -euo pipefail

PROFILE="minimal"
DEST=""

usage() {
  cat <<'USAGE'
Usage: build-fixture-repo.sh <dest> [--profile minimal|standard|multi-project|federated]

Profiles:
  minimal        empty .ddx/ (config + empty beads.jsonl), no seeded data
  standard       .ddx/ + 5 mixed-priority sample beads
  multi-project  two registered projects (proj-a, proj-b); proj-a seeded
  federated      hub/ + spoke/ projects (each ddx-initialized; federation handshake not registered)

Env:
  DDX_BIN  path to the ddx binary (default: ddx from PATH)
USAGE
}

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
  case "$1" in
    --profile) PROFILE="$2"; shift 2;;
    --profile=*) PROFILE="${1#--profile=}"; shift;;
    -h|--help) usage; exit 0;;
    --) shift; break;;
    -*) echo "build-fixture-repo: unknown flag: $1" >&2; usage >&2; exit 2;;
    *)
      if [[ -z "$DEST" ]]; then
        DEST="$1"; shift
      else
        echo "build-fixture-repo: extra positional arg: $1" >&2; exit 2
      fi
      ;;
  esac
done

if [[ -z "$DEST" ]]; then
  echo "build-fixture-repo: <dest> is required" >&2
  usage >&2
  exit 2
fi

DDX_BIN="${DDX_BIN:-ddx}"
if ! command -v "$DDX_BIN" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "build-fixture-repo: ddx binary not found (DDX_BIN=$DDX_BIN); set DDX_BIN or add ddx to PATH" >&2
  exit 3
fi

# Resolve to absolute path so cd-ing around doesn't break us.
mkdir -p "$DEST"
DEST="$(cd "$DEST" && pwd)"

# create_project <abs-dir>
create_project() {
  local dir="$1"
  mkdir -p "$dir/.ddx"
  (
    cd "$dir"
    # Scrub Git-local env vars so hook-context GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE /
    # GIT_INDEX_FILE do not redirect git operations to the outer repo.
    # This protects callers that invoke this script from inside a lefthook hook
    # or other git hook context where these vars are set by git itself.
    for _git_local_var in GIT_DIR GIT_WORK_TREE GIT_INDEX_FILE GIT_COMMON_DIR \
        GIT_OBJECT_DIRECTORY GIT_ALTERNATE_OBJECT_DIRECTORIES GIT_PREFIX \
        GIT_SHALLOW_FILE GIT_NO_REPLACE_OBJECTS GIT_REPLACE_REF_BASE \
        GIT_CONFIG GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS \
        GIT_CONFIG_COUNT GIT_INDEX_VERSION GIT_LITERAL_PATHSPECS \
        GIT_GLOB_PATHSPECS GIT_NOGLOB_PATHSPECS GIT_ICASE_PATHSPECS \
        GIT_QUARANTINE_PATH GIT_INTERNAL_NEW_OBJECT_HOOK GIT_IMPLICIT_WORK_TREE; do
      unset "$_git_local_var" 2>/dev/null || true
    done
    unset _git_local_var
    git init -q -b main
    git config user.email "fixture@ddx.test"
    git config user.name  "DDx Fixture"

    cat > .ddx/config.yaml <<'YAML'
version: "1.0"
library:
  path: .ddx/plugins/ddx
  repository:
    url: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library
    branch: main
YAML
    : > .ddx/beads.jsonl

    cat > .gitignore <<'EOF'
.ddx/agent-logs/
.ddx/workers/
.ddx/exec-runs.d/
.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*/
.ddx/executions/*/embedded/
.ddx/executions/**/scratch/
.ddx/*.lock
.ddx/*.tmp
.ddx/server.env
.ddx/server/
EOF

    git add .ddx/config.yaml .gitignore
    git commit -q -m "chore: init fixture project"
  )
}

# seed_standard_beads <abs-dir>: adds 5 mixed-priority sample beads + commits.
seed_standard_beads() {
  local dir="$1"
  (
    cd "$dir"
    "$DDX_BIN" bead create "Sample: lint cleanup"        --priority 1 --labels "phase:1,kind:chore"  --description "Pretend lint cleanup."   --acceptance "lint passes" >/dev/null
    "$DDX_BIN" bead create "Sample: add docs"            --priority 2 --labels "phase:1,kind:docs"   --description "Pretend docs write-up." --acceptance "doc exists"  >/dev/null
    "$DDX_BIN" bead create "Sample: refactor API"        --priority 0 --labels "phase:2,kind:refactor" --description "Pretend refactor."    --acceptance "tests green" >/dev/null
    "$DDX_BIN" bead create "Sample: write tests"         --priority 2 --labels "phase:1,kind:test"   --description "Pretend new tests."     --acceptance "coverage up" >/dev/null
    "$DDX_BIN" bead create "Sample: investigate flake"   --priority 3 --labels "phase:2,kind:bug"    --description "Pretend flake hunt."    --acceptance "rootcause"  >/dev/null

    git add .ddx/beads.jsonl
    git commit -q -m "chore: seed sample beads"
  )
}

case "$PROFILE" in
  minimal)
    create_project "$DEST"
    ;;
  standard)
    create_project "$DEST"
    seed_standard_beads "$DEST"
    ;;
  multi-project)
    create_project "$DEST/proj-a"
    create_project "$DEST/proj-b"
    seed_standard_beads "$DEST/proj-a"
    ;;
  federated)
    create_project "$DEST/hub"
    create_project "$DEST/spoke"
    seed_standard_beads "$DEST/hub"
    ;;
  *)
    echo "build-fixture-repo: unknown profile: $PROFILE" >&2
    usage >&2
    exit 2
    ;;
esac

echo "fixture ready: $DEST (profile=$PROFILE)"

# build-fixture-repo

Reusable clean ddx-initialized git repo for tests, integration runs, and
acceptance demos. Avoids polluting the main DDx project when an example
workspace is needed.

## Usage

```bash
scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh <dest> [--profile minimal|standard|multi-project|federated]
```

Cleanup is the caller's responsibility (`rm -rf <dest>`).

### Env

| Var       | Purpose                                                  | Default            |
| --------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------ |
| `DDX_BIN` | Path to the `ddx` binary used for seeding sample beads. | `ddx` (from PATH) |

## Profiles

| Profile         | Layout                                          | Seeded data                                  |
| --------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `minimal`       | `<dest>/.ddx/` (config + empty `beads.jsonl`)   | none                                         |
| `standard`      | `<dest>/.ddx/`                                  | 5 mixed-priority sample beads (P0–P3)        |
| `multi-project` | `<dest>/proj-a/`, `<dest>/proj-b/`              | `proj-a` seeded with the standard 5 beads    |
| `federated`     | `<dest>/hub/`, `<dest>/spoke/`                  | `hub` seeded with the standard 5 beads. The federation handshake is **not** registered — wire it up in your test if needed. |

Each project is its own git repo (`git init`, initial commit on `main`).

## Examples

```bash
# Operator walkthrough.
scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh /tmp/ddx-demo --profile standard
cd /tmp/ddx-demo && ddx bead ready

# Cross-project smoke.
scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh /tmp/ddx-mp --profile multi-project
ls /tmp/ddx-mp   # proj-a proj-b
```

## Go test helper

Tests should use `testutils.NewFixtureRepo` instead of calling the script
directly — it auto-cleans via `t.Cleanup` and lazily builds a `ddx` binary
from `cli/` if neither `$DDX_BIN` nor `ddx` on `PATH` is available:

```go
import "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/testutils"

func TestX(t *testing.T) {
    projA := testutils.NewFixtureRepo(t, "minimal")
    // ... use projA as a real ddx-initialized project root.
}
```

For `multi-project` / `federated`, the returned path is the parent dir;
sub-projects live underneath (`proj-a`, `proj-b` / `hub`, `spoke`).

Prompt-injection boundary markers are not yet explicit around artifact bodies that later flow into prompt assembly. The current server-side artifact readers in `cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:205-217` clamp text for transport, but they do not wrap the body in an explicit untrusted-data envelope for downstream prompt renderers. The review prompt path in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1561-1630` only uses XML tags for the bead envelope; it does not mark artifact content with a dedicated boundary contract.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no shared delimiter helper for untrusted artifact bodies. The current prompt builders rely on ad hoc XML wrappers and transport clamping, which are not the same thing as a prompt-injection boundary that clearly distinguishes trusted instructions from untrusted artifact text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small helper (likely in `cli/internal/evidence` or the prompt-rendering package that owns the assembler) that wraps artifact bodies in a canonical untrusted-data delimiter pair.
- Update the prompt-rendering call sites that inline artifact bodies to use the wrapper before interpolation.
- Add a regression test named `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` that asserts the delimiter pair appears around the artifact payload and that the raw body is still preserved inside the wrapper.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not alter the transport-level clamp behavior or its `truncated` metadata.
- Do not change review verdict parsing or the FEAT-022 telemetry schema in this slice.

Startup reconcile may blindly process stale desired-state or ServerState project entries such as /Users/erik/Projects/pqueue and /Users/erik/Projects/tablespec. On manual reconcile these stale entries spawned throwaway exited workers, creating noisy process churn and making restart recovery hard to trust.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/server.go:384-416 discovers desired worker project roots from server WorkingDir and persisted ServerState projects, but stale ServerState/desired-state entries can be reconciled without proving the project is present, valid, or capable of producing liveness.
- cli/internal/server/server_startup_reconcile_test.go:96-204 does not seed stale desired-state/project entries or assert that startup reconcile skips or prunes them with observable audit/error output.

PROPOSED FIX
Make startup reconcile stale-safe: desired entries for projects that are missing, no longer registered as valid, or repeatedly exit before producing liveness should be skipped or pruned with an audit/error event carrying the project path. Avoid spawning repeat exited workers for stale projects while preserving valid desired worker state.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement GraphQL worker count/cap changes from ddx-c713d75e. Do not change bead claim semantics or Fizeau routing. Do not remove valid desired worker state for active projects such as /Users/erik/Projects/ddx and /Users/erik/Projects/sesame. During the 2026-06-16 DDx canary run, `ddx worker restart worker-20260616T193804-54c3` replaced one managed DDx worker but the server briefly reported three workers for /Users/erik/Projects/ddx: the existing active attempt on ddx-7608f739, replacement worker worker-20260616T195037-f595 on ddx-6c284fb3, and an extra worker worker-20260616T195037-8dc5 on ddx-d4d764ea. Desired count for the project was two. The operator had to run `ddx worker stop worker-20260616T195037-8dc5` to restore the count. This blocks trusting the server/web UI as the primary worker manager.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/worker.go:223-244 posts a restart request for a single worker ID and does not reconcile or assert the project actual count after restart.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:2919-2929 handles POST /api/agent/workers/{id}/restart by directly calling WorkerManager.RestartWorker and returning the new worker ID without desired-count reconciliation/audit.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:2038-2067 stops the old worker and immediately starts a replacement.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:811-815 separately schedules desired-count refill after managed terminal states, and cli/internal/server/workers.go:2863-2883 can provision more workers based on fast live count. These paths can race an explicit restart and over-spawn.

PROPOSED FIX
Make explicit worker restart count-preserving for the worker project. Restart should replace the target worker without allowing the desired-state refill/reconcile path to create an additional sibling. Add audit evidence for restart start/finish and ensure status immediately after restart reports exactly desired_count running managed workers for that project.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change GraphQL StartWorker count/cap behavior from ddx-c713d75e. Do not change bead execution semantics. Long-running `ddx try` attempts can pass `--request-timeout 6h` and still be killed after 3h because that flag only controls provider request timeout, not the runner process wall-clock. Heavy release-build beads need a project-configurable absolute wall-clock cap so a serial worker can finish without hard-coding a global default.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:45` and `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:87` already consume `RunArgs.WallClock` and default to `DefaultWallClockMS`, but `cli/internal/config/types.go:447` exposes only `AgentConfig.TimeoutMS`. `cli/internal/config/resolved.go:100` populates idle timeout from `agent.timeout_ms` but never populates `ResolvedConfig.wallClock` from project config, and `cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json:291` rejects `agent.wall_clock_ms`.

PROPOSED FIX
Add `agent.wall_clock_ms` to config types, resolution, schema, and run-test constructors. Add a focused config-resolution test proving the resolved wall-clock comes from project config.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the default 3h wall-clock, provider request timeout semantics, route-resolution timeout, worker retry policy, or harness routing.","id":"ddx-0112fec3","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","kind:bug","phase:build"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":2190,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"agent: honor configured wall-clock timeout","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:36.388787019Z"}

lifecycle.go:EvaluateLifecycleQueue correctly identifies LifecycleBucketWaitingDependencies beads regardless of Status.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Remove the Status==Open check at store.go:2172.
2. Update Blocked() comment to clarify it returns beads in LifecycleBucketWaitingDependencies; the bucket designation is the single source of truth.
3. Verify Blocked() behavior is unchanged by running ReadyExecutionBreakdown breakdown tests.

NON-SCOPE
Do not refactor EvaluateLifecycleQueue. Do not change the bucket classification algorithm.

DDx has no runtime concept of a "global" plugin install nor a precedence order for resolving a plugin name. Every install/lookup path is hardcoded to the per-project tree via ddxroot.Path(). Before `--global` install (sibling bead) or `ddx init --global` (sibling bead) can exist, there must be a single resolver that, given a plugin name, returns its on-disk path by checking the project layer first, then a global layer, then the baked-in default.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/registry/installer.go:66 `InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, src, projectRoot)` only knows the project root; there is no global target.
- cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75 `Path()` resolves only the project root (in-tree `<project>/.ddx` or convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` at line 86). There is no `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global` helper.
- cli/internal/skills/install.go:28 `Install(src, projectRoot, opts)` and all callers assume a single project destination.
- No `ResolvePlugin`-style function exists anywhere under cli/internal/registry.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `GlobalDir()` to cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go returning `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global` (reuse the existing `xdgDataHome()` helper at ddxroot.go:89). The plugins root is `GlobalDir()/plugins`.
2. Add `registry.ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name string) (path string, layer string, error)` implementing precedence:
   - `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name>` if the dir exists → layer "project".
   - `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>` if it exists → layer "global".
   - baked-in default (defaultplugin.FS, the `ddx` plugin only) → layer "baked-in"; return an error for any other unresolved name.
3. Export the layer string so doctor/init/install (sibling beads) can report which layer answered.

NON-SCOPE
- Wiring `--global` into install/update commands (sibling bead: install/update --global).
- `ddx init --global` and agent-tier symlinks (sibling bead: init topology).
- `ddx doctor` reporting (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Removing the existing per-project install. Both layers coexist.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — `ddxroot.Path()` and the new `GlobalDir()` must share the same XDG_DATA_HOME convention agreed there. Sibling beads in this set depend on this resolver.","events_attachment":"ddx-04566a8f/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-04566a8f","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:config","area:plugin","initiative:zero-footprint","kind:feature","phase:3","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"queue-rank":1320,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-20de9a81","status":"closed","title":"registry: add two-layer plugin resolution (project → global → baked-in) with global XDG path helper","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:32.88452598Z"}

The `ddx-08b452ee` stale-source binary preflight now blocks `ddx work`/`ddx try` when the installed DDx binary commit is behind the DDx source checkout. During normal queue operation, DDx immediately creates tracker/evidence commits such as `chore(beads): update ...` after installing a verified build. Those commits do not affect the compiled CLI, but they make source HEAD advance past the installed binary commit. The next `ddx work --watch --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx` restart can then fail before scanning the queue, forcing meaningless rebuilds after every tracker-only bookkeeping commit.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/cmd/command_factory.go:671-715` treats any descendant commit between the binary commit and source HEAD as binary staleness. It counts all commits with `git rev-list --count <binary>..<head>` and does not check whether the diff contains CLI/build-affecting paths. `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/executions/**`, `.ddx/metrics/**`, and `.ddx/attachments/**` commits therefore trigger the same fail-fast path as real Go source changes.

PROPOSED FIX
Only report source-checkout binary staleness when commits ahead of the installed binary include DDx build-affecting inputs. Tracker, execution evidence, metrics, attachments, docs, and other non-binary bookkeeping commits must not block `ddx work`, `ddx try`, or `ddx version`. Keep the existing fail-fast behavior for Go/module/build/install changes that can change the installed CLI.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken the fail-fast behavior for real CLI source changes. Do not require operators to tag/install after tracker-only commits. Beads marked superseded-by:<Y> never close automatically when Y closes. ddx-847df529 (parent ddx-09d7d787) carries superseded-by:ddx-c2a66f45-wt-6bee7238 and a note "Do not dispatch this bead independently"; the superseder closed weeks ago but the leftover stays open and blocks its parent epic. This is a primary contributor to the stuck-queue state on the current project (RC-2 in the FEAT-010 reason taxonomy).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go Close(Y) closes only Y. It does not look for open beads X with superseded-by==Y and cascade-close them. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation now requires this cascade with a strict scope guard.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend cli/internal/bead/store.go Close(Y) with a post-close cascade step. After Y closes, scan for open beads X where supersession-by-Y is the sole remaining reason to stay open. Strict scope contract (each must hold; if any fails, surface in ddx work focus Section A, do NOT cascade):
- X is open AND (X.superseded-by == Y OR X carries label superseded-by:Y) AND Y is now closed.
- X has no other open dependents (no open bead Z with Z.dependencies referencing X).
- X has no open dependencies of its own beyond the supersession marker.
- X has attempt_count == 0.
- X has no operator notes added after the supersession marker timestamp.
- X is not currently claimed or in-progress (no live claim_id).
- X carries no manual-hold / no-auto-close / container override label.

Re-read X's current tracker state inside the same Close(Y) invocation before mutating — a concurrent worker may have changed X since the scan started.

Idempotent: re-running Close(Y) is a no-op for already-closed X.
Cycle-guarded: track visited IDs within one Close(Y) call; cascade is one hop deep. If X is a superseder for an open Z, Z's cascade is DEFERRED to a separate operator action (do not transitively cascade).
Audit: emit a superseded_close event on X with body {closed_by_cascade_of: Y, reason: "closed_as_superseded_via:Y"}. Operators can grep events to reverse.

Add operator override flag at the work command surface in a separate bead (--no-auto-supersede-close); the Close() code path always runs the cascade unless the flag has been threaded through. For this bead, accept the cascade always firing; the flag wiring is the work-loop bead's responsibility.

NON-SCOPE
- Cascading transitively beyond one hop (deferred to a future bead if ever needed).
- The work-loop wiring of --no-auto-supersede-close flag (separate bead).
- RC-3 dead-intermediate cleanup walk-up (separate bead, depends on this one).

PARENT AND DEPS

Two code paths write to .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl as part of normal operation, and neither auto-commits the result. Operators end up with persistent untracked-and-modified worktree state that contradicts the project's intent (1083 attachment files ARE tracked across 11.6 MB of committed history — this is first-class audit-trail data, not detritus).

Concrete demonstration today:

1. Run `ddx try <id>` on 9 beads → 9 new untracked .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/ dirs appear in main repo. Each contains routing decisions, cost-per-attempt, execution-routing-intent, prose-check.findings, execute-bead outcome — exactly the audit data the convention says we keep.
2. Run `ddx bead migrate` → 142 events externalized into existing tracked attachment files (showing as M), 1 new untracked attachment dir, 292 beads archived (modifying .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl), and a fresh worktree of 151 dirty files. None committed.
3. The audit log clearly shows the historical pattern: commits like `878931ce3 chore(beads): commit missing event attachments` and `5d54258fd Merge bead ... attempt ... into main` periodically sweep up the accumulation — but it's recurring manual cleanup, not the steady-state design.

ROOT CAUSE
Two distinct writers, neither stages or commits its output:

A. The execute-loop emits per-attempt routing/cost/intent events. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go emitRouteFailureEvent (line 3872) and the routing/cost emitters all call store.AppendEvent → the bead store writes to .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl when the bead's events are externalized (or to the inline events array when not). The Store.AppendEvent path has no follow-up `git add`. The execute-loop's chore("add execution evidence") commit (visible in git log as `chore: add execution evidence [<run-id>]`) stages `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` per AGENTS.md convention — but NOT `.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/`.

B. cli/internal/bead/migrate.go ArchiveWithEvents (line 561) externalizes inline events from closed/archived beads to attachments. After externalizing 142 events today across many files, the migrate command exits without staging or committing the resulting changes. The operator command surface (cli/cmd/bead.go migrate handler) has no auto-commit step analogous to the bead store's other auto-commit paths.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Execute-loop landing path: extend the existing post-merge "chore(beads): commit missing event attachments"-style commit (or the bead's own evidence commit) to ALSO stage .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/ for the bead just landed. Use the same git-index-lock recovery (cli/internal/gitlock per ddx-6a518fd9) so concurrent workers and the statusline poller do not race.
2. `ddx bead migrate` auto-commit: after a successful migrate that wrote any attachment changes (newly externalized + newly archived rows), call a single auto-commit at end-of-run with message `chore(beads): externalize and archive (<N> events to <M> attachments, <K> beads archived)`. Skip the commit entirely when no changes were written (idempotent re-run is a no-op).
3. Regression invariant: after `ddx try <id>` completes with `success`/`already_satisfied`, `git status --short` in the main repo MUST show zero untracked files under `.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/` for that bead.
4. Operator escape hatch: `ddx bead reconcile-attachments` — single command that stages every untracked .ddx/attachments/ dir and any modified attachment files, commits as `chore(beads): reconcile missing attachment commits (<N> dirs, <M> modified)`. Useful for one-shot cleanup of pre-existing accumulation when this fix lands (today's 10 untracked + 141 modified dirs are an immediate use case).

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change WHAT gets externalized (the migrate event-externalization logic is fine).
- Do not change ATTACHMENT FORMAT or location.
- Do not add attachments to .gitignore (they ARE valuable cross-machine audit data per characterization).
- Do not auto-commit .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ — that's per-machine and stays untracked under existing convention.

DEPS
ddx-6a518fd9 (landed) — required so the auto-commits route through index-lock recovery and don't bounce on operator/statusline git contention.","events_attachment":"ddx-051500ff/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-051500ff","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:bead","area:execution","area:devx","kind:bug","phase:2"],"priority":2,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-a6b20b71","status":"closed","title":"bead: auto-commit attachment writes from execute-loop and migrate","triage.power_hint":6,"updated_at":"2026-05-15T18:51:55.615439056Z","work-failed-routes":[{"actual_power":5,"at":"2026-05-15T00:39:54Z","count":1,"model":"qwen3.6-27b-autoround","provider":"bragi-club-3090","reason":"provider_connectivity"}]}

The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

The readiness producer and pre-claim intake consumer can drift on the `readiness_checks` JSON shape. That drift caused the parent incident when one side emitted `verdict` as a bool and the other side expected a string.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The pre-claim prompt specifies the expected output shape in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:616-621`, while the Go consumer decodes `ReadinessChecks` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:68` and `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:781-814`. Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/server/ (workers, federation, GraphQL adapters)
- cli/internal/config/ (resolved, types)
- cli/internal/triage/, cli/internal/metaprompt/, cli/internal/registry/
- library/checks/ (where ddx-side paths exist)

A regression linter (lefthook hook or go vet analyzer) blocks any PR that introduces a new filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...) literal outside cli/internal/ddxroot/.

SPEC IMPACT — the following docs reference .ddx/ paths and must be updated in this bead:
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md (state paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-019-multi-project-server-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-020-multi-machine-coordinator-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-021-operator-prompt-beads-web-write-path.md (operator-prompt paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/workers-sessions-operator-surface.md (worker status paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md (FEAT-010 runs/)

Each spec update: replace .ddx/<path> citations with ddxroot.Path()/<path> AND add a "project identity" subsection where applicable referencing this bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Skills/personas install strategy (principle 2 — separate bead).
- Test matrix (separate bead).
- Migrating today's in-tree ddx-repo to convention mode (out of scope; this repo stays in-tree as the dogfooding canon).

The worker still treats `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` as a recoverable no-progress outcome even when the just-created children still point back to the attempted bead. That leaves the same bead getting reclaimed forever while the children are structurally blocked.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` already has no-progress and candidate-skip machinery, but it does not check for the specific back-edge case where a fresh child carries the attempted bead in its deps. Without that guard, the worker continues to re-claim the parent instead of escalating.

PROPOSED FIX
When a run ends with `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` and any freshly-created child still depends on the attempted bead, emit a durable `operator_attention` event with reason `parent_back_edge`, park the bead, and stop re-claiming it. Add a regression named `TestWorkHaltsOnParentBackEdge` that proves the worker exits the loop rather than retrying the parent.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the dependency validation commands, doctor repair logic, prompt templates, or lifecycle docs in this bead.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:869-925` `ClaimWithOptions` persists live claim state (`status=in_progress`, `owner`, `claimed-at`, `claimed-pid`, `claimed-machine`, `claimed-session`, `claimed-worktree`, `work-heartbeat-at`) into `.ddx/beads.jsonl` before touching the external lease.

PROPOSED FIX
Move worker-owned live claim state fully into sidecar state. Interactive agents opened inside a DDx project can treat broad DDx prompts like "what's next", "what's blocking the queue", or "do work" as permission to start code edits. That competes with ddx work, which is supposed to drain execution-ready beads, and leaves no crisp default for human-required queue intervention, planning, and worker-capacity decisions.

ROOT CAUSE
AGENTS.md:1-18 enters bead policy immediately without declaring a default interactive queue-steward posture. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:18-29 defines ddx run/try/work execution layers but not the interactive-agent boundary around them. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:28-32 routes phrases like "do work" and "what's on the queue" through the ddx skill without distinguishing queue stewardship from bead execution. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 maps "What should I work on next?" to ddx bead ready, which exposes worker-ready beads rather than human-required intervention.

PROPOSED FIX
Define explicit DDx agent modes and implement the guardrails around them: queue_steward for broad interactive sessions, bead_execution for ddx work/try/execute-bead workers, direct_user_implementation for explicit human requests to edit code/docs, and review for read-only review asks. Add a focused operator command surface for intervention work and worker-capacity suggestions, while preserving ddx work as the owner of execution-ready implementation beads.

NON-SCOPE
- Replacing ddx work's existing execution loop.
- Building a full TUI or chat UI.
- Rewriting bead tracker storage by hand.
- Changing merge-policy or tracker audit semantics.

CHILD QUEUE
- Spec guidance updates define the mode taxonomy and precedence.
- DDx skill/AGENTS guidance makes queue stewardship the broad interactive default.
- Agent execution plumbing marks ddx work/try prompts with bead_execution mode.
- A command surface exposes human-required intervention and worker-capacity advice.
- Regression tests and prompt evals prove interactive guidance does not suppress ddx work execution.","id":"ddx-09d7d787","issue_type":"epic","labels":["area:agent","area:cli","area:skills","kind:epic","operator-ux","phase:2","queue-steward","spec:FEAT-010","spec:FEAT-011"],"parent":"ddx-ac10e7af","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010,FEAT-011","status":"closed","title":"agent-modes: define queue-steward execution boundary","updated_at":"2026-06-12T01:18:10.521566485Z"}
+{"closing_commit_sha":"81958ef5baab9362ad0e85b1c481c0c5a20a4c21","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:02.143711599Z","description":"# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 migration-tooling methods used by `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and related admin commands: `MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata`. These are admin/one-shot tooling, not steady-state Backend operations. After the BL bead moves concrete `*Store` to `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/`, `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` cannot reach these methods. They need an exposure path that doesn't violate the boundary lockdown.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes the 7 migration methods. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` imports `cli/internal/bead` and calls them on `*Store` concretely. Per TD-027 §11.5 + Migration row in the mutation rules table, these are admin tools not on the `Backend` interface. After BL, the only public surface in `cli/internal/bead/` is what's exported there — concrete `*Store` won't be reachable.

# PROPOSED FIX

Per the converged design recommendation (separate factory rather than `BeadMigration` sub-interface on Backend, to avoid forcing every backend to stub source-specific migrations):

1. Create `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` with a `Migrator` interface and `NewMigrator` factory:
   ```go
   type Migrator interface {
       MigrateLifecycle(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateLifecycleDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateFromHelix(ctx context.Context, src string) error
       MigrateToAxon(ctx context.Context, opts MigrateToAxonOptions) error  // deprecated; replaced by ddx-IMP
       MigrateDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired(ctx context.Context) (bool, MigrationReport, error)
       ReconcileLifecycleMetadata(ctx context.Context) (ReconcileReport, error)
   }

   func NewMigrator(opts MigratorOptions) (Migrator, error)
   ```
   The concrete implementation lives in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` after BL; the public factory returns a `Migrator` interface.

2. Move the 7 methods' bodies from `*Store` (in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `migrate.go`) onto an unexported `*storeMigrator` type that holds a `*Store` reference and implements `Migrator`. The `NewMigrator` factory returns this.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and any related admin commands:
   ```go
   mig, err := bead.NewMigrator(bead.MigratorOptions{Dir: workspace})
   if err != nil { ... }
   mig.MigrateLifecycle(ctx, opts)
   ```

4. Remove `*Store.MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata` from the `*Store` public method set. Logic moves to the `*storeMigrator` type.

5. `MigrateToAxon` retained as a deprecated method on `Migrator` that returns `ErrDeprecated` with a pointer to the IMP bead's new importer. Final removal happens after IMP lands.

# NON-SCOPE
- The new JSONL→Postgres importer (replaces `MigrateToAxon`'s JSONL-writing behavior) — separate IMP bead.
- Schema-versioning migration ladder framework — deferred until first breaking change is proposed.
- Other concerns — separate beads.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` declares the `Migrator` interface (7 methods, ctx-aware) and `NewMigrator(opts) (Migrator, error)` factory. Compile-time assertion: `var _ Migrator = (*storeMigrator)(nil)`.
2. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 migration methods directly. Verified by `grep -E 'func \(s \*Store\) (MigrateLifecycle|MigrateLifecycleDryRun|MigrateFromHelix|MigrateToAxon|MigrateDryRun|DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired|ReconcileLifecycleMetadata)\b' cli/internal/bead/*.go` returning nothing.
3. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` constructs `Migrator` via `bead.NewMigrator(...)` and invokes operations. Holds `Migrator` interface, not `*storeMigrator` concrete.
4. End-to-end CLI invocation works: `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --dry-run`, `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply` produce identical output to today. Test: `TestMigrator_LifecycleMigration_RoundTrip`, `TestMigrator_FromHelix_PreservesAllFields`.
5. `MigrateToAxon` on the Migrator returns `ErrDeprecated` with message pointing at the new importer (will be implemented in IMP). Test: `TestMigrator_MigrateToAxon_ReturnsDeprecated`.
6. Existing migration tests in `cli/internal/bead/migrate_test.go` continue to pass against the new Migrator-mediated path.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

+{"acceptance":"After `ddx install <name> --force --local <path>` succeeds, `ddx installed` reports STATUS=ok with VERSION matching the source's plugin.json `version` field and INSTALLED set to today's date.","closing_commit_sha":"rev-ddx-0b332070","created_at":"2026-05-18T02:12:47.205384969Z","description":"## Summary

Observed against ddx v0.6.2-alpha103-12-ge6379ebc5 on 2026-05-18.

After `ddx install helix --force --local /home/erik/Projects/helix` succeeds (output: 'Linked .ddx/plugins/helix -> /home/erik/Projects/helix; Installed helix from local path; recorded plugin pin unchanged'), `ddx installed` reports:

```
NAME   VERSION  TYPE      STATUS  INSTALLED
helix  0.3.3    workflow  BROKEN  2026-05-12
```

Three things are wrong:
1. STATUS=BROKEN despite the install reporting success.
2. VERSION=0.3.3 despite the linked source's plugin.json declaring 0.4.1.
3. INSTALLED=2026-05-12 (date of the prior successful install) — not updated by today's --local install.

The 'recorded plugin pin unchanged' message in the install output is the smoking gun: DDx doesn't update the version pin when installing from --local. It leaves the registry at whatever the last registry install pinned, even though the on-disk plugin is now at a different version.

## Expected behavior

After a successful `ddx install --local`, `ddx installed` should:
- Report STATUS=ok (not BROKEN) if the install succeeded.
- Report VERSION matching the plugin.json of the linked source.
- Report INSTALLED date of today (the actual install).

## Workaround

None known. The metadata is misleading but the file content is correct, so runtime works.","events_attachment":"ddx-0b332070/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0b332070","issue_type":"bug","labels":["kind:bug","area:install","area:installed-registry","observed-failure"],"owner":"easel","priority":2,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"ddx installed reports BROKEN status after a successful --local install that creates a symlink-style plugin","updated_at":"2026-05-28T16:52:13.877578439Z"}

Some worker failure paths still call `Store.Unclaim`, which updates the bead row and then removes the claim heartbeat outside the same critical section. ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1081-1100 implements `Unclaim` as row update followed by separate heartbeat removal.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1103-1130 implements `Release`, which atomically updates the row and clears the heartbeat under one lock.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2365-2368 still uses `w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID)` on a worker refresh failure path; other worker paths should be audited for the same pattern.

PROPOSED FIX
- Audit worker-owned failure/unclaim paths and replace them with `Release` where the current worker owns the claim.
- Add a fault-injection test that makes heartbeat removal fail and proves the bead is not left with a fresh sidecar lease after a worker failure path releases it.
- Preserve CLI/operator `ddx bead update --unclaim` semantics where direct `Unclaim` is still appropriate.

NON-SCOPE

Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- The orchestrator's 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning, when emitted alongside the parent-back-edge state, is upgraded from WARN to ERROR with the offending bead ID logged. Test: dispatch_logs_error_on_root_mutation_with_back_edge.
- Lifecycle schema documents that 'parent' and 'deps' MUST be disjoint and that 'deps' MUST be acyclic with the parent-tree. docs/ddx/lifecycle-rules.md (or equivalent) updated; ADR file if no governing artifact already exists.
- Existing valid graphs continue to pass 'ddx bead doctor' without --fix and without regression — regression test against a pre-existing tracker snapshot.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. ROOT CAUSE
  cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go (package routinglint) currently forbids retired compensating-routing identifiers (ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef, etc.) and flag literals (--escalate, profile_ladders). It does not yet forbid direct field assignment of Profile/Harness/Model on Runtime structs inside run/try/work command code paths. The SD-024 runtimelint analyzer (cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer.go:41) forbids durable-knob fields on *Runtime structs generically, but does not name a per-command allowlist or produce a test that verifies the run/work/try paths specifically. No TestRoutingLint* test exercises this invariant.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extend routinglint (cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go) to flag direct Profile/Harness/Model field assignment on types matching *Runtime in cli/cmd/ run/work/try command files, or add a new rule in runtimelint covering this.
  - Alternatively, add a targeted integration test TestRoutingLint_RunPathNeverSetsProfileDirectly that runs the analyzer against the live tree and asserts zero diagnostics for the assignment pattern.
  - Fix any existing violations: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1893-1897 passes overrides.Profile/Harness/Model through CLIOverrides to config.LoadAndResolve — verify this is compliant (CLIOverrides is not a Runtime struct).

NON-SCOPE
  - Changes to agent_run or agent_run_overrides command behavior.
  - Changes to the fizeau or ladder routing logic.
  - Lint rules for non-routing fields on Runtime structs (covered by runtimelint).","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"{"detail":"description drops commitment \"cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1893\"","intake_outcome":"ambiguous_needs_human"}","created_at":"2026-05-08T06:33:09.032244684Z","kind":"intake.blocked","source":"ddx agent work","summary":"ambiguous_needs_human"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"","created_at":"2026-05-08T15:24:50.040104278Z","kind":"lifecycle_reconciled","source":"ddx bead reconcile","summary":"expired retry-after metadata no longer blocks execution"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"from_status=open

When a `ddx work` orchestrator dies abnormally (SIGKILL, crash, OOM, or supervising-process loss) while an execute-bead attempt is running, the spawned harness child can reparent to PID 1 and continue indefinitely. Observed 2026-05-27: a `claude --print` execute-bead child remained orphaned with PPID 1 for ~9h53m after its `ddx work` parent exited.

ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go:156 spawns the harness via `exec.Command(binary, args...)` and then calls `cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd)`.
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:13-15 sets only `SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}`. There is no Linux `Pdeathsig`, so the kernel does not signal the harness when the parent dies abnormally.
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:20-25 kills the process group only from graceful executor paths.
- cli/cmd/root.go:49-54 uses `signal.NotifyContext` for os.Interrupt and SIGTERM only. SIGKILL and crashes cannot be caught, so executor cleanup does not run.

PROPOSED FIX
On Linux, set `SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig` to SIGKILL or SIGTERM for execute-bead harness commands in `cmdSetProcessGroup`. Handle the Go runtime thread-migration caveat with `runtime.LockOSThread` around process start or an equivalent parent-PID guard so the parent-death signal is attached to the intended parent thread. Preserve process-group setup so existing graceful cancellation and timeout cleanup still kill the whole harness tree. Document the macOS/Windows fallback because `Pdeathsig` is Linux-only.

NON-SCOPE
- Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown already works through executor.go:231-255; do not regress it.
- The harness-runner-internal leak in fizeau is covered by fizeau-50b6f055; this child bead is only the DDx orchestrator-to-harness-child boundary.
- Startup orphan reaping is covered by a separate child bead from this decomposition.
- docker-clone backend cleanup is covered by a separate child bead from this decomposition.

CROSS-REF
- fizeau-50b6f055: fizeau harness-runner process-group cleanup.
- ddx-8f2e0ebf: lease release / operator-attention.","events_attachment":"ddx-0d7d5f0e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0d7d5f0e","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability","severity:high"],"parent":"ddx-93d2ac52","priority":0,"queue-rank":1080,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-f490694e","status":"closed","title":"fix executor: enforce parent-death cleanup for execute-bead harness children on Linux","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:31.74819903Z"}

Running `make install` from a clean DDx checkout builds and installs a binary stamped with `-dirty` even when no source edits were pending. During the 2026-05-15 monitor restart, `make install` reported `v0.6.2-alpha81-63-gffe356c1b-dirty`, and `git status --short` showed only `cli/internal/server/frontend/build/.gitkeep` modified.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/Makefile:24-31` runs `bun run build`, which empties `cli/internal/server/frontend/build/`, then recreates `internal/server/frontend/build/.gitkeep` using `touch`. The tracked `cli/internal/server/frontend/build/.gitkeep` currently contains a newline byte, but `touch` recreates an empty file after Vite removes the directory contents. That one-byte mismatch makes the source checkout dirty before `go build` computes `git describe --dirty` in `cli/Makefile:4-6`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Normalize the tracked placeholder `cli/internal/server/frontend/build/.gitkeep` to the same empty-file content produced by the build target, or otherwise make `frontend-build` recreate the exact tracked bytes.
- Verify `make install` from a clean checkout leaves no tracked `.gitkeep` diff and installs a DDx binary whose version string does not end in `-dirty`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not track generated frontend build output under `cli/internal/server/frontend/build/`.
- Do not change the frontend bundle, Vite config, or go:embed target.
- Do not bypass the stale-binary guard in `ddx work`.

DEPS
No deps. +{"closing_commit_sha":"bf453dbf9706daeae151b744b128a1e2753a1e5b","created_at":"2026-06-02T18:09:06.405724115Z","description":"PROBLEM
Pre-merge checks turn an attempt-context cancellation or an infrastructure failure into a quality BLOCK, stranding good work as preserved-not-landed. Observed signature: pre-merge check "production-reachability" failed to start: context canceled -> Blocked. cli/internal/checks/runner.go runOne classifies failed-to-start, ctx-cancel, AND a check that ran-but-crashed / wrote no result all as StatusError; cli/internal/agent/checks_premerge.go (~143) maps StatusError->Blocked=true. So a cancelled attempt (or a local-box check that cannot start) blocks landing of changes the agent correctly produced.

PROPOSED FIX (fail-open ONLY on unconfirmed infra; a crashed check still blocks)
1. Add checks.StatusInfra to cli/internal/checks/protocol.go.
2. In runner.go runOne: ONLY ctx.Err()!=nil (cancellation) and the failed-to-start branch (the non-ExitError case where the command could not launch) return StatusInfra. A check that DID start but exits non-zero, or wrote no result file, STAYS StatusError (indistinguishable from a real failure — a crashing security/secrets check must not silently land bad code). A check declares "inconclusive" only by writing a result file with explicit StatusInfra, never by crashing.
3. In checks_premerge.go: set Blocked on StatusBlock OR StatusError; StatusInfra is NON-gating (surface it as an advisory/report, do not preserve on it).

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e

Successful `ddx work` landings still leave `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/result.json` modified in the operator checkout after the worker exits or self-replaces. This happened twice during the current monitor run: `ddx-8a0aea8e` left `.ddx/executions/20260515T154605-7c45a217/result.json` dirty with `outcome` changing from `task_succeeded` to `merged`, and `ddx-7e422ff6` left `.ddx/executions/20260515T161644-6d9781bd/result.json` dirty the same way. In both cases the operator had to create an extra `chore: finalize execution result ...` commit manually. That violates the no-dirty-root invariant for normal worker success.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1582-1588` documents that the worker writes a preliminary `result.json` before committing so the implementation commit message can be sourced from tracked artifact data. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:850-868` commits the evidence bundle during landing, but that commit happens before the orchestrator result rewrite. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2605-2626` then rewrites `result.json` in the project root with final fields such as `implementation_rev`, `landed_rev`, `landed_branch`, `outcome=merged`, and `orchestrator_status`. No follow-up staging/commit path captures that final rewrite, so the tracked `result.json` remains dirty after otherwise successful landings.

PROPOSED FIX
After `WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact` rewrites a tracked execution `result.json` in the project root, stage and commit just that final result artifact (or fold it into the evidence commit by moving the final rewrite before evidence commit) using the same git/index-lock recovery path as other DDx-managed audit commits. The final branch history must contain the final orchestrator result fields and `git status --short` must be clean for that attempt.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` only if final-result timing belongs in landing
- Focused execute-bead landing tests under `cli/internal/agent/`

NON-SCOPE
- Do not track embedded session logs or `.ddx/run-state/**`.
- Do not change the result JSON schema.
- Do not remove the preliminary result write used for commit-message provenance unless the replacement still preserves that contract.

ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder rejects the readiness payload when a readiness_checks entry's `verdict` is a JSON bool, emitting `decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string`. Under warn-only policy this skips every bead, idling a full fizeau queue (23 ready beads, 2026-05-18 05:27-05:39 UTC). The identical error fingerprint (15155a1c...) across distinct bead_ids confirms a producer/consumer schema mismatch, not a bead defect.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go` — `preClaimReadinessCheck.Verdict` (struct at :75) bound `verdict` to a plain string, so `decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResultWithMode` (:688) and `decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult` (:681) fail when the readiness producer emits `verdict:true`. A verdict may legitimately be a JSON bool, a JSON string (e.g. "fail"/"pass"/"ready"), or be omitted.

PROPOSED FIX
Give the field a dedicated `readinessVerdict` type (:91) with a custom UnmarshalJSON that accepts a JSON bool (coerced to a documented canonical lowercased string), a JSON string (trimmed), and JSON null/absent (empty), and rejects any other JSON kind with a descriptive error. Downstream comparisons in `failedReadinessReasons` (:823) and `readinessCheckEvidence` (:848) operate on the canonical lowercased string.

NON-SCOPE
Does NOT add the shared JSON schema or CI drift gate (covered by the schema-lock child). Does NOT change the worker same-fingerprint escalation (escalation child), the rolling claim-success-rate signal (claim-rate child), or README docs (docs child). Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable. Test: ddx_doctor_detects_and_repairs_back_edge.
- ddx work no-progress remediation: when an attempted bead's outcome is 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale' AND any freshly-created child carries the attempted bead in its deps, ddx auto-emits an 'operator_attention' event with reason 'parent_back_edge' and stops re-claiming that bead. (Today it loops indefinitely.) Test: ddx_work_halts_on_parent_back_edge.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events_attachment":"ddx-130cf9ab/events.jsonl","execution-eligible":false,"id":"ddx-130cf9ab","issue_type":"task","labels":["ac-quality:needs-refinement","area:decomposition","area:lifecycle","area:tracker","decomposed","kind:correctness","kind:safety"],"parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"queue-rank":1800,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"Reject bead dep edges that re-introduce the parent or any ancestor: part 2","updated_at":"2026-06-17T20:06:21.005257886Z"}

Convention mode (per ddx-ad338ec3) bootstraps an XDG project dir but has no source for skills, personas, or the default ddx plugin. Either every project re-downloads the plugin on first use, or the user must have a global install. The latter is the better answer: install once per machine, all convention-mode projects share.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no per-user install path today. `ddx init` only writes per-project state. Skills/personas live in the project's .ddx/plugins/ (in-tree) or are missing entirely (convention).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `ddx init --global` flag (and corresponding code path in cli/cmd/init.go). When set:
   - Resolve target path: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ (defaults to ~/.local/share/ddx/global/).
   - Install the default ddx plugin (library/ contents) into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.
   - Write a default ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (library URL, branch, etc.).
   - Idempotent: re-running upgrades to a newer plugin version but does not destroy operator state.

2. Convention mode reads ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ as the second precedence tier (in-tree > global > hardcoded). If --global has never been run, fall back to baked-in hardcoded defaults — convention mode still works without it; --global is an optimization for users who want offline-cacheable plugin content.

3. Document the install path in ddx doctor: a new check "Global Install" reports whether ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ exists and points operators to `ddx init --global` if it does not.

4. Do NOT remove the existing per-project plugin install (in-tree mode at .ddx/plugins/<name>/). Both coexist.

NON-SCOPE
- In-project ddx init manifest write (E4 bead).
- Migration from legacy ~/.config/ddx/ or ~/.ddx/ if such exist on disk (none expected; today there is no global install).
- Plugin update / upgrade flow for the global install (use existing `ddx update` mechanism; verify it works against the new path).

DEPS
ddx-564ea1cf (E1) — resolver must exist.

During the 2026-06-16 DDx repo canary, durable audit recorded an index.lock cap violation while committing DDx-managed audit outputs after a successful worker land. .ddx/metrics/locks.jsonl shows operation=index.commit by holder_pid=4149669 acquired at 2026-06-16T21:26:33.947089425Z, violation duration_ms=10002, cap_ms=10000, and release duration_ms=10013. The lock was eventually released and no stuck .git/index.lock remained, but this still violates the Lock Lifetime Contract: index.lock hold p99 and individual holds must stay under the 10s cap.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go:139-142 commits every dirty DDx-managed durable audit path in one git commit under runDurableAuditGitWithIndexLockRecovery, which can hold .git/index.lock long enough to cross DefaultIndexLockCap.
- cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go:224-229 derives the durable-audit git timeout from the index.lock cap, but the observed metrics prove the subprocess can still reach the cap boundary and emit a violation before returning.
- cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockcap.go:22-24 defines DefaultIndexLockCap as 10s, and lockmetrics recorded the violation rather than proving durable audit stayed below that cap.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:809-830 documents the intended evidence-commit contract: cap-bounded commits should cancel/recover stale locks rather than let the watchdog force-release a still-running git process. Durable audit needs the same no-violation proof.

PROPOSED FIX
Change the durable-audit commit path so ordinary audit commits do not run up to or past DefaultIndexLockCap. Prefer a bounded approach that either avoids committing large transient audit payloads under index.lock, splits/limits durable audit commits, or times out before the cap with clean stale-lock recovery and operator-visible evidence. Preserve committed audit evidence and do not drop required attempt metrics, bead events, or attachment manifests.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change worker desired-count semantics, startup reconcile, plugin topology, or Fizeau routing. Do not hide lock violations by raising the default cap. Do not remove durable audit evidence from git history to make the metric disappear.

PARENT
- ddx-d5dfa702

DEPENDENCIES

When newer code is installed (the factory merges fixes + `make install`), the worker keeps executing STALE logic until manually killed+restarted. It only WARNS ("installed ddx is built from X; source HEAD is Y (N commits ahead); run make install") — no action. Observed live: a 2-worker drain ran ~75 commits behind, missing ALREADY-LANDED concurrency fixes (ddx-33530e68 post-land-outside-lock, ddx-0b659710 atomic-claim-release, ddx-274943fc worktree-leak) and repeatedly colliding on the durable-audit commit — the very fixes were sitting in source but not in the running binary. Operators must manually cycle workers. Operator expectation: "the workers are supposed to know how to cycle automatically between tasks." (A stopgap supervisor that runs `ddx work --once` + `make install` between cycles is in place; this bead is the durable native fix.)

ROOT CAUSE: the --watch loop loads its binary once at startup; there is no between-task self-refresh / re-exec. The staleness warning (grep "installed ddx is built from" / "source HEAD") is informational only.

PROPOSED FIX: At a SAFE boundary in the --watch loop — after a bead completes/unclaims and BEFORE claiming the next — detect whether the on-disk installed binary differs from the running process's build commit (compare the embedded build commit vs the installed binary's reported commit, or hash/stat the executable resolved via os.Executable/PATH). If newer, re-exec the process in place (syscall.Exec) on the fresh binary, preserving argv/flags (--watch, --min-power, etc.) so the next bead runs on the latest code. NEVER re-exec mid-bead. Gate behind --self-refresh (default ON for --watch). This delivers "workers cycle automatically between tasks."

NON-SCOPE: triggering `make install`/the build from the worker (separate concern — a supervisor/build pipeline produces the binary; this bead re-execs onto it); the durable-audit collision classifier (ddx-83361480/e8bf0695/2965925e).","events_attachment":"ddx-171dc478/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-171dc478","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:workers","incident:stale-binary-collision","kind:bug","kind:reliability","phase:iterate","spec:FEAT-010"],"parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"queue-rank":1840,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-0d9fc756","status":"closed","title":"ddx work --watch: self-cycle between tasks — re-exec on a newer installed binary so workers never run stale code","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:35.036561504Z"}

The operator had to stash/restore files by hand before the queue could continue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:575-599` treats any non-DDx dirty implementation path as a hard pre-dispatch checkpoint refusal. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1880-1912` converts that refusal into `operator_attention`, unclaims the candidate, logs “commit or clean”, and returns from the work loop. That behavior is safe for arbitrary user WIP, but it is not robust for DDx-managed watch drains where leaked lifecycle/root edits or generated artifacts must be preserved and the worker should keep draining.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace the hard stop with an audited preserve-and-clean path for pre-dispatch dirt in managed `ddx work --watch` flows: write a named preservation artifact or git stash/ref containing the dirty patch, record the dirty paths and preserve handle in the attempt/work log, restore the affected paths to HEAD, unclaim the candidate without losing the patch, and keep the watch loop alive so the next scan can proceed. The implementation must still avoid silently discarding operator WIP: the preserve handle and affected paths must be visible in events/log output and the root must be clean before dispatching the next bead.

NON-SCOPE
Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. Do not weaken checkpoint filtering for `.ddx` bookkeeping paths. Do not squash/rewrite execute-bead history. `ddx work` and `ddx try` refuse to start when run from inside the DDx source repo and HEAD has commits ahead of the installed binary that touch `cli/`, `Makefile`, or `install.sh`. The operator sees a usage dump and a "installed ddx binary is stale for this DDx source checkout" error; nothing executes. This makes `ddx work --watch` unusable in an active-development workflow where the source moves often.

The hard fail was introduced by ddx-08b452ee with the rationale "do not silently run stale code during execution". But:
- It only fires inside the DDx source checkout (origin matches `DocumentDrivenDX/ddx`), so production users never see it.
- The in-loop `buildWorkBinaryRefreshCheck` (`cli/cmd/work_binary_refresh.go`) already exits + re-execs when the installed binary changes mid-loop. So the staleness gap is bounded to "until the next `make install` is run", not "forever".
- The startup gate strictly subsumes a runtime warning: there is no scenario where the runtime check would let stale code run that the startup gate catches.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace the hard fail in `cli/cmd/work.go:133` and `cli/cmd/try.go:120` with a call to `warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource` (print the existing warning to stderr, return nil). ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder once failed to unmarshal the readiness_checks response when readiness_checks[].verdict arrived as a JSON bool, because the producer (readiness prompt) emitted bool while the consumer (Go decoder) parsed string. Under warn-only policy every claim attempt produced an identical-fingerprint intake_error warn and the worker idled the whole fizeau queue (23 ready beads, one daemon) indefinitely. The detail string was: "decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string". This child owns the schema-gate slice of parent ddx-61f6c57d: make the readiness payload shape a single enforced contract so this class of producer/consumer drift fails the build instead of silently wedging the queue at runtime.

ROOT CAUSE
The verdict shape contract lives in two places that can drift independently: the producer prompt string assembled in buildPreClaimIntakePrompt (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:639-642) and the Go consumer readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:97-125), which coerces bool true->"pass", bool false->"fail", string->lowercased, null/absent->"". The canonical shape is declared in cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json (readiness_checks[].verdict is oneOf boolean/string/null). Without a CI gate binding all three together, a change to any one side reintroduces the wedge.

PROPOSED FIX
Keep cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json as the single source of truth for the readiness payload. Ensure (a) the schema accepts exactly the verdict forms readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON accepts, (b) the producer prompt continues to cite readinessChecksSchemaPath (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21,639-642) as authoritative, and (c) a CI gate fails on drift: the `readiness-schema` Makefile target (cli/Makefile:152-156) runs the schema tests and is invoked from .github/workflows/ci.yml:66-67 via `make readiness-schema`. The gate must fail if the schema, the prompt, or the decoder diverge on the verdict contract.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement the same-fingerprint warn escalation / silent-idle guard — that is the sibling child (worker loop escalation). Do not change the warn-only policy_mode semantics. Do not implement any other parent acceptance criterion. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint.","events_attachment":"ddx-1d071ef5/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-1d071ef5","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:pre-claim","decomposed-child","kind:bug","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability","severity:high","triage:no-changes-unjustified"],"parent":"ddx-fd02cf6e","priority":0,"queue-rank":1140,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-500716b9","status":"closed","title":"Enforce shared readiness_checks schema gate in CI so producer prompt and Go decoder cannot drift on verdict type","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:32.042521375Z","work-failed-routes":[{"actual_power":5,"at":"2026-05-28T01:46:59Z","count":3,"endpoint":"http://bragi:1234/v1/chat/completions","model":"qwen3.5-27b","provider":"bragi","reason":"provider_connectivity","timeout_class":"bad gateway"}],"work-no-changes-count":2}

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1073-1113` claims the candidate selected from the queue snapshot without a post-selection guard that rechecks execution eligibility after tracker changes made by a prior attempt. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1681-1696` passes the originally selected `candidate` into `agenttry.Attempt`, so a stale candidate can still execute even if the store row has since been parked. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2367-2408` appends the terminal result and continues watch mode after a preserved/land-conflict/no-change result, allowing an immediate retry. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2603-2627` delegates selection to `PreviewQueue` and `ReadyExecution`, but the live incident proves this path can still select or retain an execution-ineligible decomposed parent. `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:131-134` documents the intended invariant: successful decomposition creates children and sets the parent `execution-eligible=false`.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an execution-eligibility recheck immediately before claim and again before invoking `agenttry.Attempt` in the watch/drain loop. If a candidate is now execution-ineligible, decomposed, superseded, or otherwise absent from the fresh execution-ready view, release/skip it without dispatching an implementer and continue to the next ready bead or idle. Ensure preserved no-change decomposition and mixed no-change/land-conflict outcomes do not allow the same live watcher to retry the parent in the same loop run.

- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go

- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go, only if needed for fixture reuse

NON-SCOPE
Do not change merge policy or preserved-iteration storage. Do not re-enable execution of decomposed parents. Do not hide land conflicts generally. Do not close the parent automatically unless existing child-closure policy already authorizes it.

None.
