`ddx work --watch` can reject every pre-claim readiness result when `readiness_checks[].verdict` is emitted as a JSON bool instead of the consumer's expected string shape. In the observed Fizeau queue, each ready bead produced the same `pre_claim_intake.warn` decode error and the worker idled indefinitely despite ready work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The readiness classification decoder consumes `readiness_checks` through `preClaimReadinessCheck.Verdict` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:75-80`, then uses `failedReadinessReasons` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:823-834` to interpret failed checks. The contract must explicitly tolerate bool, string, null, and absent verdict values at this boundary; otherwise producer/consumer schema drift turns a readiness payload into `intake_error` before a bead can execute.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement or verify a custom `readinessVerdict` decoder that accepts `true`, `false`, strings, `null`, and absent verdicts, coercing to a documented canonical form (`true` -> `pass`, `false` -> `fail`, string -> trimmed string, null/absent -> empty). Ensure the classification decode path exercises that type and that failed-check detection still treats canonical `fail` as the failure signal.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Fizeau route or harness configuration. Do not implement same-fingerprint escalation, claim-success-rate warnings, CI schema locking, README documentation, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not rely on `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/agent-logs/...` as load-bearing test input; inline fixtures in tests. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF
AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.

  Land() in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go invokes git fetch
  origin, git push --ff-only, and a push-race auto-recovery loop as part of
  the core drain lifecycle. This couples Land() to remote I/O, holding
  withMainGitLock for 30-120s on remote round-trips, which produced the
  observed contention failures (ddx-08e90869, ddx-15a51f8c). Per the user's
  architectural position and the sister doc-only bead amending FEAT-010,
  Land() must perform ONLY the local worktree-merge into local main; origin
  sync (fetch) and push are separate operations, never invoked from Land()
  or the drain loop.

  This bead removes all network I/O from Land() and from the LandingGitOps
  interface. Origin-sync and push become operator responsibilities (raw
  git commands today; a future ddx sync helper is downstream of multi-node
  work and explicitly out of scope per operator direction).

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:27-29 file-header comment
    declares "If an origin remote exists — push the new target tip" as
    step 5 of the canonical Land flow.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1036-1043 Land() wraps the
    entire landLocked() call under withMainGitLock, including push.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1146-1216 landLocked merge
    path issues git fetch origin (FetchBranch), then git push (PushFFOnly),
    then on push race calls landPushAutoRecover.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1479 landPush triggers
    landPushAutoRecover on non-FF rejection.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1538-1580 landPushAutoRecover
    runs fetch + temp-worktree merge + update-ref + push, all inside the
    same lock.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:239-297 LandingGitOps
    interface includes FetchBranch, PushFFOnly, and FetchOriginAncestryCheck
    methods that exist solely to support the in-Land network I/O path.
  - LandResult fields PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered, and the
    fetchOriginError type all exist to communicate network-I/O outcomes
    that Land() should no longer be responsible for.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Strip Land() to local-only operations:
      a. fetch-origin call (FetchBranch) at the start of landLocked:
         REMOVE.
      b. FetchOriginAncestryCheck before merge: REMOVE (the merge proceeds
         on local main as-is; if origin diverged, that is discovered when
         the operator pushes, by raw git push rejecting non-FF, which is
         the operator's problem).
      c. PushFFOnly at end of landLocked: REMOVE.
      d. landPushAutoRecover function: REMOVE entirely.
  - Update LandingGitOps interface:
      a. Remove FetchBranch method.
      b. Remove PushFFOnly method.
      c. Remove FetchOriginAncestryCheck method.
      d. Keep merge / update-ref / worktree / commit methods.
  - Update LandResult:
      a. Remove PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered fields.
      b. Remove fetchOriginError type.
      c. Keep merge / commit / preserve outcomes.
  - Update the file-header comment at lines 27-29 to reflect the
    network-free flow.
  - Update Land() critical section: withMainGitLock now wraps only the
    local-index slice (merge-or-update-ref + evidence commit + worktree
    sync). Sub-second hold expected.
  - Existing tests for push-race recovery (and any LandResult.PushFailed

During the current monitor on 2026-05-15, two Codex execute-bead workers recovered from avoidable tool-sequencing errors. For ddx-b6859802, the worker staged ordinary files and ignored evidence with parallel git add calls; one failed with: fatal: Unable to create ... index.lock: File exists. For ddx-bb8e7d07, the worker ran lefthook run pre-commit before staging and got a no-staged-files skip, then had to rerun after staging; it also attempted to interrupt a completed test-poll session. These recoveries waste worker time and can look like DDx dirty-tree failures even when the implementation is otherwise fine.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2337-2341 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2369-2373 tell rich-prompt and embedded-agent workers to run tests, stage specific paths, and commit once. They do not explicitly prohibit parallel git/index operations, do not say that lefthook run pre-commit must be run after staging when hooks are staged-file-sensitive, and do not warn that a no-staged-files hook run is insufficient acceptance evidence.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update both executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText and executeBeadInstructionsAgentText to require git/staging/commit commands to run sequentially, not through parallel tool calls.
- Require workers to rerun lefthook run pre-commit after staging the exact commit set when pre-commit depends on staged files; an earlier no-staged-files hook run is not sufficient AC evidence.
- Add or extend prompt guardrail/snapshot tests in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go or cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_prompt_snapshot_test.go so the rule cannot regress.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change core gitlock recovery.
- Do not change execute-bead lifecycle, result schema, or merge policy.
- Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl.

# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md:510 defines US-101 for starting server-managed ddx work capacity.

PROPOSED FIX

- Add cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/workers-dispatch.spec.ts covering count=2 startWorker, workers.max_count refusal, stop worker, lifecycle/audit visibility, and live update behavior.
- Reuse or extend existing frontend E2E fixture helpers instead of relying on the developer live DDx state.

+{"acceptance":"1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestIsTransientGitContention|TestFinalizeDurableAudit.*TrackerLock' -count=1` passes.
2. A unit test asserts `isTransientGitContention` returns true for an error wrapping `TrackerLockTimeoutError`.
3. A unit test asserts `isTransientGitContention` returns true for the literal substring "tracker lock timeout" as a defense-in-depth fallback.
4. A unit test of `finalizeDurableAuditOrStop` injects a tracker-lock-timeout error and asserts: (a) `result.OperatorAttention` is NOT set, (b) the durable_audit_transient event is emitted, (c) the function returns false (don't stop).
5. `cd cli && go test ./... -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"f5ad95d750e192a6579517c58ab83d69c1216ec0","created_at":"2026-05-29T02:21:39.52547268Z","description":"PROBLEM: When two or more `ddx work` workers run concurrently against the
same project and worker B reaches `FinalizeDurableAudit` at the same moment
that worker A holds the tracker lock, worker B times out, and the loop
classifies the timeout as `operator_attention` and exits — even though
nothing requires operator action. The other worker just needs to finish.

OBSERVED 2026-05-28 in niflheim drain with two concurrent workers:
- Worker1 (pid 424293) was committing tracker state for niflheim-996afcc8.
- Worker2 (pid 681801), simultaneously finalizing niflheim-e43f04bb (an
  ADR-051 cold-tier PUT concurrency implementation that had already run to
  completion — 66091 tokens, 32m8.5s), hit `tracker lock timeout (max
  elapsed, lock: /home/erik/Projects/niflheim/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock,
  owner pid: 424293)` while trying to commit durable audit outputs.
- Worker2 exited with `operator attention required`, releasing
  niflheim-e43f04bb back to `open` without committing the audit trail,
  and leaving these paths dirty: `.ddx/beads.jsonl`,
  `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`, `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl`,
  `.ddx/attachments`.

The work was successful — 32 minutes of agent time produced a clean
implementation and commit. The lock race nuked the audit attribution and
forced the worker to stop. Re-running it would either repeat the agent run
(burning more tokens) or hit `already_satisfied` (with the worktree-leak
documented in `ddx-274943fc`).

ROOT CAUSE: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1262-1309`
(`finalizeDurableAuditOrStop`) calls `runtime.FinalizeDurableAudit` and
branches on `isTransientGitContention(err.Error())` to decide retry vs
operator-attention. The matcher at
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1019-1034` covers `.git/index.lock`
(via `gitlock.IsIndexLockError`) and the strings "unable to write new index
file", "cannot lock ref", "unable to update the ref" — but **does not cover
the DDx-specific tracker-lock error format** emitted by
`cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:243`:

  "tracker lock timeout (%s, lock: %s, owner pid: %s)"

The tracker lock is a DDx-internal ordering lock around tracker writes
(`.ddx/.git-tracker.lock`), held briefly during tracker-state mutations.
Concurrent worker contention on this lock is structurally identical to
the `.git/index.lock` case the existing matcher handles, and the comment
on `isTransientGitContention` explicitly states the design intent:

  "A persistent cause (e.g. ENOSPC) simply keeps releasing+retrying the
   bead, which is still preferable to halting the whole unattended drain."

PROPOSED FIX: Extend `isTransientGitContention` to recognise the DDx
tracker-lock-timeout string class. Recommended implementation:

  1. Export a stable marker constant (or recogniser) from
     `cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go` rather than string-matching
     across packages. Suggested: a sentinel error type
     `TrackerLockTimeoutError` wrapping the existing fields (lockDir,
     ownerPID), with an `Is(err)` helper.
  2. Update `FinalizeDurableAudit` and its callers to wrap the timeout
     error with this sentinel, so callers can use `errors.Is` instead of
     substring matching.
  3. In `isTransientGitContention` at `execute_bead_loop.go:1019`, add a
     branch that returns true for `errors.Is(err, TrackerLockTimeoutErr)`.
     Keep the existing substring fallbacks for defense in depth.

DDx has a live bead-store split-brain across bead commands, work planning, and targeted execution. On 2026-06-13 after installing 75d5c4e84, from /Users/erik/Projects/ddx-provider-failure-20260613: `ddx work` executed and closed JSONL-backed bead ddx-81e3adcd, and `rg ddx-81e3adcd .ddx/beads.jsonl` finds it at .ddx/beads.jsonl:316, but `ddx bead show ddx-81e3adcd --json` returns `bead: not found`. Conversely, `ddx bead show ddx-b53eb508 --json` sees a newly filed bead, but `ddx try ddx-b53eb508` returns `bead not found`. Operators cannot trust queue state, cannot target priority work, and cannot force provider/process failure bugs to the top if read/write/execution paths observe different stores.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- cli/cmd/bead.go:291-322 resolves bead commands through CommandFactory.beadWorkspaceRoot() and beadStore(). In this worktree it resolves to a store that sees ddx-b53eb508 but not JSONL bead ddx-81e3adcd.
- cli/cmd/work_plan.go:82-91 uses f.beadStore() for planning, but live `ddx work plan --json` still listed JSONL-backed ddx-81e3adcd while `ddx bead show` could not inspect it.
- cli/cmd/try.go:177 constructs `bead.NewStore(resolveBeadStoreRoot(projectRoot))`; live `ddx try ddx-b53eb508` failed with `bead not found` despite `ddx bead show ddx-b53eb508 --json` succeeding from the same cwd. Additional try call sites at cli/cmd/try.go:263, :384, :444, and :515 also construct stores separately.
- cli/cmd/shared_helpers.go:41-48 is the shared root helper, but commands are still mixing beadWorkspaceRoot(), resolveBeadStoreRoot(projectRoot), and direct ddxroot.JoinProject-style paths elsewhere.

PROPOSED FIX

Centralize bead store resolution for bead show/list/ready/export/update/create, work plan/work picker, work status/focus, and try behind one project-aware resolver that returns both the selected state path and a diagnostic explaining why. Add regression coverage for a repository with both an in-tree .ddx JSONL store and an alternate configured/global store so show, ready, plan, work, and try all see the same bead IDs and lifecycle state. Ensure `ddx try <id>` can execute every bead visible in `ddx bead show <id>`, or fails with a typed store-mismatch diagnostic that includes both physical store paths.

NON-SCOPE

Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl. Do not remove support for alternate bead backends. Do not rewrite execute-bead history. Do not treat this as only a display bug; command behavior must converge.","events_attachment":"ddx-231dff55/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-231dff55","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:beads","area:work","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","store-resolution","plan:server-managed-workers"],"notes":"2026-06-13 dogfood rerun of ddx-ef9df563 failed in ExecuteBeadWithConfig before dispatch: result.json reported 'loading bead ddx-ef9df563 from worktree snapshot: bead: not found'. Under multi-worker operation, a ddx work worker EXITS with "operator attention required: DDx could not commit durable audit outputs; resolve the git failure before continuing autonomous work" when the durable-audit/tracker commit hits transient .git/index.lock contention. Observed 2026-05-27: with 2 ddx workers + a fizeau worker + the server sharing one project .git, a worker died on `git add` tracker -> ".git/index.lock: File exists ... no owner found but lock is fresh (age 19s < 30s)". A transient lock conflict stops the whole worker; the more workers, the more often this fires, directly defeating unattended steady-state drain.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- git .git/index.lock carries no pid, so gitlock.IndexLockOwner finds no owner. RecoverGitIndexLock (cli/internal/gitlock/gitlock.go:144-158) only reaps an ownerless lock once age >= StaleAge (30s, gitlock.go:26); below that it returns Removed=false ("no owner found but lock is fresh").
- RunGitWithIndexLockRecovery retries only RecoveryAttempts=3 (gitlock.go:30) with LiveOwnerWait=500ms (gitlock.go:34) ~= 1.5s total, far short of the 30s reap floor, then returns the index.lock error.
- commitTrackerLocked (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2153) calls runGitWithIndexLockRecovery for `git add`; its error becomes "staging tracker: ...". That propagates to the durable-audit-commit exit at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1175, which stops the worker (operator attention) instead of treating transient lock contention as retryable.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Do NOT exit the drain on transient .git/index.lock contention during the durable-audit/tracker commit. Classify index.lock contention as transient (mirroring the pre-claim tracker-contention class from ddx-df77e668), release the bead, and continue the loop so the next iteration retries. A transient lock must never stop a worker.
2. AND/OR raise the recovery patience for tracker/audit commits to a bounded backoff that can outlast typical contention (up to ~the StaleAge window) before failing.
3. Optional: reap a provably-orphaned lock sooner (e.g. confirm no live git holds the repo via lsof) while keeping the 30s floor when liveness is unknown.
Recommend (1) as the primary fix: transient lock contention is retryable, not operator-attention-worthy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not weaken safety against removing a live git process index.lock.
- Do not change the landing-path read-tree retry (already has backoff, ddx-7e659c95).

CROSS-REF: same multi-worker-resilience theme as ddx-57c40485 (lock scoping) and ddx-df77e668 (pre-claim tracker-contention). This is the post-fix gap on the COMMIT side.","events_attachment":"ddx-23ac2796/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-23ac2796","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","observability","prevention:ddx-57c40485","prevention:ddx-df77e668","priority:reliability","severity:high"],"notes":"Already fixed by operator direct-fix (bootstrap-hostile to dogfood): transient index.lock during durable-audit commit now classified retryable via gitlock.IsIndexLockError, worker continues instead of exiting. Landed 48631b2ed; TestWork_IndexLockContentionDuringAuditCommitIsTransientNotFatal + regression pass; full internal/agent gate green; reinstalled.","priority":0,"queue-rank":1010,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: worker exits on transient index.lock contention during durable-audit commit instead of retrying — kills unattended multi-worker drain","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:31.462993595Z"}

A server-managed worker must be fully cleanable. Stop, watchdog reap, restart, and server shutdown must not leave ddx work, claude, codex, shell, or sleep descendants behind.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:192 says WorkerManager manages in-process work workers as goroutines. cli/internal/server/workers.go:438 creates a cancelable context, but no OS process boundary. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1234 reads WorkerRecord.PID and :1282-1286 terminates a process group only when pid > 0. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1453-1457 has the same limitation in watchdog reap. Because server-owned workers are often goroutines with PID zero, the server cannot guarantee child harness cleanup.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce a server-managed process boundary. Preferred implementation: spawn a child ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> in its own process group and record PID/PGID in WorkerRecord. If a transitional in-process path remains for tests, every harness child process group must be registered on the worker handle and killed through the same cleanup helper. Stop/reap/shutdown should send SIGTERM, wait WatchdogKillGrace, SIGKILL remaining descendants, release bead claims, persist terminal status, and be idempotent.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not change bead claim semantics except releasing claims for the stopped/reaped managed worker. Do not implement UI controls.","events_attachment":"ddx-24a3da84/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-24a3da84","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:server","area:workers","kind:feature","kind:reliability","phase:build","plan:server-managed-workers"],"parent":"ddx-9d1af129","priority":0,"queue-rank":90,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"IP-2026-06-13-server-managed-workers","status":"closed","title":"server: terminate managed worker process trees without leaks","updated_at":"2026-06-15T16:57:07.750082289Z"}

outcome (`provider_connectivity`, `no_evidence_produced`, `execution_failed`,
`structural_validation_failed`, `land_conflict`, `post_run_check_failed`),
its execute-bead worktree under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<attemptID>/`
is not removed. Cleanup happens only when a *subsequent attempt of the same bead*
is launched and `RecoverOrphans` runs as pre-spawn sweep.

OBSERVED 2026-05-28 in niflheim drain: a 6.6 GB worktree
`.execute-bead-wt-niflheim-07f6f8ff-20260528T164209-f179349f` remained 9+
hours after its attempt failed with `provider_connectivity` (bragi 502).
The bead's retry resolved as `already_satisfied` without spawning a new
execute-bead worktree (the AC was already met by partially-landed commits
from the failed attempt), so `RecoverOrphans` was never called for that
bead — the leak is permanent until another attempt at the same bead runs.

The user reports seeing this pattern consistently across drains, not just
on the one observed bead.

ROOT CAUSE: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:393-411`
defines `RecoverOrphans` with the docstring "the parent's responsibility —
call it before spawning new workers so stale worktrees from crashed previous
attempts do not accumulate." The function matches strictly by
`basenamePrefix := ExecuteBeadWtPrefix + beadID + "-"` and is only invoked
on a new-attempt pre-spawn path. Two leak conditions follow:

  (a) Bead's retry succeeds via `already_satisfied` (no new worktree
      spawned because the AC check resolves before agent invocation).
      The orphan from the prior failed attempt is never matched.
  (b) Bead is closed for any reason after a failed attempt and is never
      re-attempted (most common in real flows — failures often resolve
      via decomposition into different child beads, never returning to
      the original).

The post-attempt outcome classifier at
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1966` (Classify worker outcome:
task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes / ...) is the natural place
to call `WorktreeRemove` for non-success classes, but does not.
The existing landing path at
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1088,1329,1679` calls
`gitOps.RemoveWorktree` correctly for landing-time temp worktrees, but
those are different (per-landing) worktrees, not the per-attempt execute-bead
worktree under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.

PROPOSED FIX: Add an attempt-finalize cleanup hook that runs after outcome
classification (around `execute_bead.go:1966-2017`) and removes the
per-attempt execute-bead worktree when the outcome is anything other than
`task_succeeded`. Preserve only when:
  1. The operator explicitly opted in via `ddx try --no-merge` (preserve
     for inspection regardless of outcome).
  2. An `executions_mirror.go` write or evidence-capture step requires the
     worktree to persist past classification (verify by reading the call
     chain at `execute_bead.go:2069-2114`).

Implementation steps the executing agent should follow:
  1. Read `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1423-2017` to confirm the
     outcome-classification flow and identify the right post-classify hook
     point.
  2. Add a helper `cleanupAttemptWorktree(gitOps, workDir, wtPath, outcome,
     preserveFlag)` that calls `gitOps.WorktreeRemove(workDir, wtPath)` for
     non-success outcomes unless `preserveFlag` is set.
  3. Wire it into the outcome-classification path.
  4. Add a unit test asserting the cleanup branch for each non-success
     outcome class, and a negative test confirming success-path keeps the
     worktree (landing path owns that cleanup).
  5. Update `RecoverOrphans` docstring to reflect that it's now a
     belt-and-suspenders sweep, not the primary cleanup path.

NON-SCOPE:
- Changing `RecoverOrphans` behavior or call sites (keep it; it covers
  hard-crash cases the new hook can't reach).
- Adding a periodic background sweeper or `ddx doctor` flag for stale

- Changing the worktree path under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.
- Changing outcome-classification logic or outcome taxonomy.
- Adding telemetry/metrics for leak rates.

IN-SCOPE FILES:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (add the cleanup hook + helper)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_test.go or sibling _test file (add

- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (docstring update on

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:

ExecuteLoopSpec fields are declared in parallel structs across three independent code sites. When a flag or worker mode is added to cobra, the developer must also update the REST handler request struct in server.go and the GraphQL adapter request struct in graphql_adapters.go; missing one silently drops the field at runtime. The new work-loop contract requires one vocabulary across CLI parsing, worker persistence, REST, and GraphQL: mode plus idle_interval, with no public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag.

ROOT CAUSE
Each work field traverses six independent declaration points with no compile-time linkage:

1. cobra flag extraction plus local vars: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 extracts fromRev, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once/watch mode, idleInterval, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel, maxCostUSD, requestTimeout, minPower, and maxPower as naked locals.

2. positional-param pass-down: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1744-1773 closes over these locals and assembles config.CLIOverrides by hand; no single struct groups them.

3. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec definition: cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is hand-maintained in parallel with cobra and has historically missed fields.

4. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker request struct: cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 is a separate anonymous struct, so newly added fields can be silently lost for server-spawned workers.

5. StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/server.go:2441-2455 plus cli/internal/server/workers.go:345-361 hand-assembles the worker spec; any field absent in step 4 cannot appear here.

6. GraphQL adapter request struct plus StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141 is a third parallel declaration; fixes to REST can leave GraphQL broken.

The consumer (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-926 runWorker) reads MaxCostUSD from escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD hardcoded (line ~753) instead of spec.MaxCostUSD because the field was never plumbed through. OpaquePassthrough has two validation skip gates that must both fire (workers.go:357-361 and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch in runWorker).

PROPOSED FIX
- cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go: define ExecuteLoopSpec with all fields currently spread across the six layers. Fields: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion int. JSON tags drive both REST wire format and disk persistence.
- Mode is the canonical once/drain/watch selector. CLI --once maps to Mode=once. CLI --watch maps to Mode=watch. Default is Mode=drain.
- IdleInterval is meaningful only for Mode=watch. --idle-interval without --watch is a usage error in the cobra layer and must not be represented as a separate polling mode in the persisted spec.
- Do not add, accept, document, or emit a public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag. Historical worker records may be read best-effort as unknown/legacy input, but new CLI/server/API inputs use Mode plus IdleInterval.
- Add Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults(). Add parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) as the single cobra-to-spec conversion.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605: replace naked-local extraction with parseExecuteLoopSpec. Remove positional-param closure pattern; pass spec by value.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49: replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with (or alias to) ExecuteLoopSpec from the new package. Delete the parallel struct.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409: delete the anonymous request struct in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker; decode directly into ExecuteLoopSpec. Call ApplyDefaults() then Validate() before StartExecuteLoop.
- cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141: delete the parallel anonymous struct and parallel StartExecuteLoop literal; route through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as REST.

A server-managed ddx work canary for ddx-cf0cafef ran Claude TUI, which executed `go test ./...` inside its isolated attempt worktree. A cli/cmd test launched a nested `ddx work`/Codex process. The nested process inherited the parent worker's DDX_PROJECT_ROOT=/Users/erik/Projects/ddx and DDX_SERVER_MANAGED_WORKER_ID=worker-20260615T174545-1e44, so it claimed and reported against the real DDx project instead of the test/attempt context. This polluted project status with agent-loop-* liveness and temporarily dirtied tracked .ddx execution files.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/executor.go:162 built provider subprocess environments from internalgit.CleanEnv(), which removes GIT_* only and preserved parent DDx routing/control variables. cli/internal/agent/script.go:239 used the same inherited base env for script harness shell commands. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1103-1108 sets attempt-specific DDX_MODE/DDX_BEAD_ID/DDX_ATTEMPT_ID and must explicitly republish DDX_WORKER_ID once inherited parent DDX_WORKER_ID is scrubbed.

PROPOSED FIX
Scrub DDX_PROJECT_ROOT, DDX_AGENT_NAME, DDX_SERVER_MANAGED_WORKER_ID, and inherited DDX_WORKER_ID from provider subprocess and script harness base environments. Preserve attempt correlation by explicitly adding runtime.WorkerID as DDX_WORKER_ID when present.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change resolveProjectRoot semantics for ordinary CLI use. Do not change server-managed worker process env used by the ddx work parent process. Do not modify the ddx-cf0cafef feature implementation attempt.","id":"ddx-292c2aa9","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:agent","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","plan:server-managed-workers","canary"],"parent":"ddx-9d1af129","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"agent: scrub server-managed worker env from provider subprocesses","updated_at":"2026-06-15T18:36:06.047776533Z"}

So the resilience added for the durable-audit commit (ddx-23ac2796, and pending ddx-83361480/ddx-e8bf0695) does NOT protect the tracker-commit call-site.

ROOT CAUSE: the tracker commit at execute_bead.go:2286 (and its caller) returns the git error directly as a hard failure; it does not classify "unable to write new index file" / index.lock / "signal: killed" as transient nor retry, unlike the durable-audit path's finalizeDurableAuditOrStop + isTransientGitContention (execute_bead_loop.go:1026,1288). Two workers committing tracker state to the same shared .git/index contend, and the loser fails its bead instead of retrying.

PROPOSED FIX: Route the tracker commit through the SAME transient-contention handling as the durable-audit commit — classify index-write/index.lock/ref-lock/SIGKILL/deadline contention (via the shared isTransientGitContention, extended by ddx-83361480) as transient and retry on a later iteration (the tracker commit should be idempotent — re-deriving and re-committing the same staged state), rather than failing the bead. Prefer a single shared resilient-commit helper used by BOTH the durable-audit path and the execute_bead.go:2286 tracker path so the two cannot diverge again.

NON-SCOPE: the durable-audit classifier/deadline (ddx-83361480, ddx-e8bf0695 — this bead reuses that classifier); worktree/file leaks (ddx-b320e794, ddx-274943fc).","events_attachment":"ddx-2965925e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2965925e","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:execution","area:git","incident:durable-audit-collision","kind:bug","kind:reliability","phase:iterate","prevention:ddx-23ac2796","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021"],"parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"queue-rank":180,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-d658e1a2","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: 'committing tracker' commit path lacks transient-retry on index contention — fails beads under 2-worker load","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:27.941899392Z"}

ddx work currently emits "No execution-eligible beads in the queue" and exits when the only ready beads are epics with open children, mislabeled closure candidates, superseded leftovers, dead intermediates, or epic-of-epic chains. Operators have to manually triage every blocker class. This violates the factory rule that ddx work must self-unstick (reliability principle P10, just added).

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:122-167 (post-edit) and docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md "Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation" subsection (just added) now mandate diagnose+auto-remediate semantics. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1263-1315 today only reads ReadyExecutionBreakdown into result.NoReadyWorkDetail, then sleeps/exits — no diagnoser, no remediation dispatch. cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go:218 isOrdinaryEpicContainer conflates openChildCount with totalChildCount, mislabeling 6 of 10 ready epics in the current project as containers when they are actually closure candidates.

PROPOSED FIX
Build the diagnose+auto-remediate idle path per FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation: per-bead Diagnose function with the closed reason taxonomy; remediator handlers for RC-1 (lifecycle classifier fix), RC-2 (supersession cascade in Close), RC-3 (dead-intermediate cleanup), RC-6 (auto-epic-decompose wrapping runPreClaimDecomposer); idle-path wiring in execute_bead_loop.go that runs diagnoser, fires matching remediators, re-scans without sleeping on success; operator surface (ddx work focus) two-section output that NEVER silently hides auto-remediable beads; ddx work plan --explain/--json flags for inspection. All safety guards (attempt cap, cost budget, cooldown, cycle guard, operator overrides, audit events) per the spec.

NON-SCOPE
- Building the FEAT-004 epic-scoped worker mode (dedicated branch/worktree, sequential child execution). Explicitly deferred in the FEAT-004 update.
- Replacing the operator-invoked bead-breakdown skill. It stays the right tool for top-down planning from a fresh spec/plan.
- Two-worker concurrency redesign — current tracker serialization is reused.

CHILD QUEUE
- diagnose: per-bead reason classifier (cli/internal/bead/diagnose.go)
- RC-1: classifier split openChildCount/totalChildCount (cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go)
- RC-2: supersession cascade in Close() (cli/internal/bead/store.go)
- RC-3: dead-intermediate cleanup walk-up in Close() (cli/internal/bead/store.go)
- RC-6: auto-epic-decompose handler (cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go)
- idle-path wiring: diagnose+dispatch+rescan in execute_bead_loop.go:1263
- work focus: two-section output (cli/cmd/work_focus.go)
- work plan: --explain/--json flags (cli/cmd/work_plan.go)","events":[{"actor":"","body":"closed_because: all_children_terminal

  The pattern "park bead to proposed status" is duplicated 6 times in agent/*.go:
  - execute_bead_loop.go
  - execute_bead_post_review.go
  - execute_bead_review_terminal.go
  - execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go
  
  Each duplication performs the same sequence:
  1. Park the bead to status=proposed
  2. Update extra metadata (reason, findings ref, etc.)
  3. Append triage/recovery event
  4. Update labels
  
  This violates DRY and makes it harder to maintain consistent behavior across 
  the agent codebase.

PROPOSED FIX
  Extract a Store.ParkToProposed helper method covering:
  1. Status transition: in_progress → proposed
  2. Label updates (optional additional labels)
  3. Event appending with consistent event body
  4. Extra metadata updates (reason, recovery ref, cost, etc.)
  
  Refactor all 6 call sites in agent/*.go to use the helper instead of duplicating 
  the pattern.
  
  The helper should accept parameters for:
  - Bead ID
  - Reason (for event body and extra metadata)
  - Optional labels (overlaid on defaults)
  - Optional extra fields (merged into existing extra)
  - Context/tracing fields (session_id, attempt_id, etc.)

NON-SCOPE
  - Changing the park-to-proposed behavior itself
  - Changing when/why beads are parked (that's in execute-bead logic)
  - Adding new persisted statuses
  - Refactoring other common patterns","events_attachment":"ddx-2af07fa2/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2af07fa2","issue_type":"task","labels":["ac-quality:needs-refinement","area:agent","area:bead","kind:refactor","phase:6","spec-id:TD-031"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":750,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-67d214f6","status":"closed","title":"extract Store.ParkToProposed helper for 6 agent duplicates","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:30.405788839Z"}

Without `--global`, install and update must target the project's ddx root, which differs by mode: in-tree (`<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>`) versus convention (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>`). Either way the project-tier agent skill links (`<project>/.claude/skills` and `<project>/.agents/skills`) must point into whichever plugins tree is active.

ROOT CAUSE
- `resolveInstallDirs(global=false)` (cli/cmd/install.go:184, project branch install.go:195-197) sets `installRoot = ddxroot.Path(ctx, f.WorkingDir)` and skill dirs to `<workingDir>/.agents/skills` and `<workingDir>/.claude/skills`. `ddxroot.Path` (cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75) returns the in-tree `.ddx` path when `<project>/.ddx` exists and the convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` path otherwise, so mode selection is implicit.
- `installLocal` non-global branch derives `ddxRootDir := ddxroot.Path(...)` (install.go:453) for the same routing.
- Update: the non-global path `performUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:204) uses `ddxroot.JoinProject(workingDir, ...)` (e.g. update.go:227) so it updates the project install in whichever mode is active; mode is not an explicit flag but follows `ddxroot`.

PROPOSED FIX
Ensure `ddx install <name>` (no `--global`) in in-tree mode writes to `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/` and links `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` and `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`; in convention mode (no `<project>/.ddx/`) writes to the XDG project plugins path and links the project-tier skill dirs into that path. Ensure `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.

NON-SCOPE
- The `--global` install/update surface (slice 2).
- `ddx init` behavior and doctor reporting (slice 4).
- Removing either mode — in-tree and convention coexist.

DEPS

Skills/personas/plugins can live in two install layers (project-scoped under ddxroot.Path() and machine-wide under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/). Runtime callers need a single authoritative resolver that picks the right on-disk plugin directory with a defined precedence, otherwise the dual-location install surface has no consumer and project installs cannot shadow global ones.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:22 ResolvePlugin must implement precedence project → global → baked-in. The project path is filepath.Join(ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot), "plugins", name) (resolve.go:23); the global path is filepath.Join(ddxroot.GlobalDir(), "plugins", name) (resolve.go:28); baked-in (defaultplugin.FS()) is valid only for name == "ddx" (resolve.go:33). ddxroot.GlobalDir() is defined at cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:90.
- Before this layer existed, install/update targeted ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and there was no precedence rule, so an operator with both a project and a global copy had undefined resolution.

PROPOSED FIX
1. ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name) returns (path, layer, err): when both project and global copies exist, return the project path with layer "project"; when only the global copy exists, return its path with layer "global"; when neither exists and name == "ddx", return ("", "baked-in", nil) so callers fall back to defaultplugin.FS(); otherwise return a non-nil error naming all three layers.
2. The project path resolves through ddxroot.Path() so it is correct in BOTH in-tree mode (<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>) and convention mode (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<identity>/plugins/<name>).
3. SPEC IMPACT: file/update the skill-install-topology ADR (docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-025-or-equivalent) documenting the three-layer precedence, and update the CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section to state the project-over-global-over-baked-in lookup precedence.

NON-SCOPE
- Does NOT remove the per-project install path (both layers coexist).
- Does NOT migrate any legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Does NOT add agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.
- Writing the plugin content / creating agent-tier links is handled by the install and init child beads, not here.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — project path routes through ddxroot.Path() and the global path through ddxroot.GlobalDir(); both must be agreed-on. Governing epic: ddx-602ae31a (Principle 2: skills/personas install-to-agent vs install-in-project).","events_attachment":"ddx-2bb4e0a5/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2bb4e0a5","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:plugin","area:registry","initiative:zero-footprint","kind:feature","phase:3","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"queue-rank":1390,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-ce0e5820","status":"closed","title":"registry: plugin lookup precedence resolver (project → global → baked-in) + topology spec","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:33.165800793Z"}

Dogfood on 2026-06-14 while executing ddx-3141b561 exposed a provider-supervision failure. The worker route was Claude (`claude/sonnet`), but the Claude-run attempt repeatedly spawned Codex and Gemini provider CLIs as direct children of the `ddx work` process: `/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/codex --no-alt-screen` on TTYs and `/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/gemini` with Node grandchildren. DDx status sometimes surfaced Codex as a provider child, but DDx did not continuously quarantine it and did not surface Gemini in status. The operator manually killed multiple Codex/Gemini process groups during one successful bead. A routed DDx execution must not depend on, leak, or silently fan out to unrelated provider CLIs whose local configuration may be absent or incompatible.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:313-330` only calls `reapSupersededProviderChildren` when a route is resolved. Provider CLIs spawned after route resolution are not continuously checked during the long running phase.
- `cli/internal/agent/provider_children.go:100-123` reports provider children to status, but non-route children remain observational unless another route-resolution or attempt-end cleanup happens.
- `cli/internal/agent/provider_children.go:157-170` can reap superseded providers, but it is only invoked at route-start rather than as a watchdog while the active route is running.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1105-1123` captures a process baseline and performs cleanup only after `attemptBackend.Run` returns. If a provider keeps itself alive by spawning provider CLIs or background monitors, DDx waits for the provider instead of enforcing the route boundary.

PROPOSED FIX

Add a running-phase provider child guard owned by `ddx work`/`ddx try`: after the active route is known, periodically scan descendants of the worker process, terminate provider CLIs whose provider Depends on the escalation-owner contract + the shared classifier (fizeau-44e98499).","events_attachment":"ddx-8d6af771/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-8d6af771","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:workers","kind:feature","spec:FEAT-004","phase:1","triage:no-changes-unjustified"],"notes":"SELF-CONTAINED SPEC (the ddx-side FEAT-004 here is Beads; the ROUTING policy lives in the fizeau repo, NOT accessible to a cold ddx agent — so the normative failure-classification table is inlined below; implement against THIS, do not look for a ddx FEAT-004 routing addendum):

Map each ddx-work attempt outcome to exactly one action:
- success / already_satisfied -> close bead
- no_changes (agent produced nothing + rationale) -> close or unclaim per rationale; do NOT escalate tier
- genuine implementation/capability failure (tests fail after real work; AC unmet) -> escalate middle->max ONCE
- dirty worktree / partial write -> clean/reset worktree, same-tier retry (not escalation)
- provider transport failure (i/o timeout, conn refused, 5xx) -> same-tier reroute to another dispatchable candidate; no tier change
- quota exhausted (retry_after) -> GLOBAL pool cooldown/sleep until reset; NOT per-bead mutation, not escalation
- auth/config missing, toolchain/setup failure -> operator-attention (typed bead state); do not escalate
- timeout / no-progress watchdog -> clean + same-tier retry once, then operator-attention
- merge/land conflict -> unclaim + retry later; not escalation
- max-tier genuine failure -> operator-attention (stop escalating)

operator-attention = explicit bead state transition (not a notification/indefinite wait). The single middle->max escalation owner is ddx work; a per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap prevents multi-worker stampede. Scope tests to avoid hangs (the prior attempt hit a 3m test timeout — bound any test that spawns/loops).","priority":0,"queue-rank":1290,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-f5db2b63","spec-id":"FEAT-004","status":"closed","title":"routing P1: ddx applies shared failure-classification table; single-owner one-hop middle->max escalation","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:32.746455781Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

2. `cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./cmd` passes without launching any new claude, codex, or gemini subprocess from the cmd test binary. Verify with a process-tree check during the run or a test-owned fake service assertion.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes with the staged files from ddx-7952776a (`cli/cmd/work.go`, `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go`, `cli/cmd/work_test.go`, `cli/cmd/work_resource_exhausted_test.go`, `cli/cmd/zero_config_work_test.go`).
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run "TestWork_CtrlC_ExitsCleanlyNoUsageNoRawError|TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim|TestZeroConfigWork_NoConfigDoesNotEmitUnderSpecified" -count=1` still passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-06-13T19:56:50.332851396Z","description":"PROBLEM

The staged pre-commit Go hook blocks small cli/cmd changes by running the entire cmd package with race enabled, and that package currently fails on unrelated tests. During ddx-7952776a verification, `lefthook run pre-commit` invoked `scripts/lefthook-go-test.sh ./cmd/execute_loop_shared.go ./cmd/work.go ./cmd/work_resource_exhausted_test.go ./cmd/work_test.go ./cmd/zero_config_work_test.go`; the script mapped those staged files to `go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./cmd`. After fixing two provider-spawning tests, the command no longer leaked provider CLIs, but still failed on unrelated command-suite tests.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `scripts/lefthook-go-test.sh:8-20` maps any staged cli/cmd file to package `cmd` and runs the full package, so unrelated cmd tests gate every small command edit.
- `cli/cmd/bead_doctor_test.go:100-101` fails under the hook with missing XDG project bead storage: `open .../xdg/ddx/projects/local/.../beads.jsonl: no such file or directory`.
- `cli/cmd/doctor_housekeeping_test.go:50-63` expects stale execution evidence counts/archive, but the hook run reports stale execution dirs=0 and leaves old evidence in place.
- `cli/cmd/skills_test.go:46-49` expects `validated 2 skill files`, but the hook run reports `validated 1 skill files`.
- `cli/cmd/update_test.go:193-197` expects `.ddx/update-backup` to exist, but the hook run fails opening that directory.
- `cli/cmd/work_durable_audit_test.go:162-163` expects dirty tracker/metrics status after durable audit commit failure, but got empty status.
- `cli/cmd/work_startup_cleanup_test.go:128-157` expects old execution evidence to archive/delete, but the hook run leaves the old evidence directory and reports zero archived/deleted dirs.

Observed earlier in the same verification: `TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim` and `TestZeroConfigWork_NoConfigDoesNotEmitUnderSpecified` started `/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/codex --no-alt-screen` through Fizeau discovery; those two tests were patched in commit 97b5ba4c8, but the broader hook still fails on the tests listed above.

PROPOSED FIX

Make the staged command hook usable as a reliable gate. Either isolate/fix the failing cmd tests so `go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./cmd` passes without provider discovery or host-specific state, or narrow `scripts/lefthook-go-test.sh` so staged-file hooks run only the affected tests/packages while a separate full-suite CI gate covers all cmd tests. Prefer fixing tests that are supposed to be hermetic.

NON-SCOPE

Do not weaken the ddx-7952776a interrupt behavior or remove its focused regression tests. Do not hide provider CLI launches; if another cmd test starts claude/codex/gemini during unit tests, stub it and add coverage that the test is hermetic.","id":"ddx-8d75f750","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:cmd","area:test","kind:bug","reliability"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":"0","schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"cmd: make staged Go command hook hermetic and green","updated_at":"2026-06-13T20:14:02.346504689Z"}

A 500 (rather than a GraphQL error inside a 200 body) means something in the artifacts resolver chain PANICS and the HTTP layer converts it to 500.

ROOT CAUSE (identified candidate): cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifact_types.go:44-48 — attachArtifactTypeDefinitionsToArtifacts() loops project roots calling artifactTypeLoader.Load(root); it checks the returned err but does NOT check whether `index` is nil, then dereferences `index.Types` at line 48. A nil index (now, or after a future loader change) panics with a nil-pointer dereference. attachArtifactTypeDefinitionsToArtifacts is invoked from the Artifacts() query resolver at cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifacts.go:184 (resolver entry at resolver_artifacts.go:92). More generally, any panic anywhere in the Artifacts() chain becomes a 500 instead of a structured GraphQL error.

PROPOSED FIX:
1. Reproduce the actual 500: issue the `artifacts(projectID: ...)` GraphQL query (see frontend query in cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.ts) against a registered project and capture the panic/stack from the server.
2. Add a nil-index guard after Load() in resolver_artifact_types.go (return a wrapped error, never dereference a nil index).
3. Fix the specific panic the repro surfaces, and confirm the gqlgen panic-recovery path returns a structured error (HTTP 200 + errors[]) rather than 500.

Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 4 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).

Verified by an integration test that injects a hanging route-resolution stub and asserts release within timeout+10s.
2. A progress watchdog fires when phase-empty heartbeats (harness="", model="", route="") continue beyond a phase-specific budget; the worker releases the lease and emits operator-attention. Verified by a test that drives heartbeats with empty fields for budget+epsilon and asserts release.
3. Claiming a bead atomically sets status=in_progress in the same write; releasing on failure atomically clears the claim. ddx bead status reflects the lease state. Verified by a multi-process test that inspects status mid-claim.
4. ddx bead operator-attention surfaces every lease release caused by timeout or wedge detection, with bead-id, attempt-id, last_activity_at, and a short diagnosis string.
5. Same-bead-twice wedge guard: if the worker observes the same bead wedge on consecutive claims, it stops claiming that bead and surfaces it for operator review; tested by injection.
6. Documentation: ddx README or AGENTS.md names the timeout defaults, the wedge-detection contract, and how to inspect ddx bead operator-attention.","closing_commit_sha":"7c4a01c1e1dd4da78bbacae26f943699847b3c5b","created_at":"2026-05-18T03:32:10.992846346Z","description":"PROBLEM
ddx work --watch can claim a bead, fail to resolve a route, and hang in the route-resolution step indefinitely. Heartbeats keep emitting (phase=running, last_activity_at advancing every 30s) but harness/model/route fields stay empty and no LLM call ever happens. The bead's lease is held but no work progresses, and the worker cannot pick up any other bead because it is single-threaded.

OBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-18 02:05–03:27 EDT)
- Worker: PID 560430 (ddx work --watch, no flags, cwd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau).
- Claimed bead: fizeau-86f2bac0 (bench-pr-A3b) at attempt_id 20260518T020552-18b086b2.
- 80+ minutes of heartbeats logged in .ddx/agent-logs/agent-loop-1779069470069950671.jsonl, every 30s, all with phase=running, harness="", model="", route="".
- ddx bead status shows In progress: 0 — the state machine never marked it in_progress despite the lease.
- ddx bead ready returned 30 dispatchable beads — none would be picked up because the worker is single-threaded and wedged.
- Related earlier event on fizeau-856b7957: routing-intent event with routing_intent_degraded=true, routing_intent_note='actual route facts unavailable'. Strongly suggests the same wedge: route resolution returns 'no route' but the worker treats it as 'wait forever' instead of 'release bead with error'.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
1. Lease without progress: holding a lease for 80+ minutes with no LLM call, no commit, no resolved route is indistinguishable from a crashed worker, but heartbeats keep ticking so the staleness reaper (if any) never fires.
2. Single-worker starvation: 30 ready beads, one stuck worker, queue stops advancing.
3. State inconsistency: bead has a claim but bead status reports 0 in_progress, suggesting the claim and the lifecycle status are not atomically coupled.
4. No actionable error surface: the operator only learns about the wedge by tailing agent-logs and noticing the empty harness/route fields. The watch loop's stdout shows nothing because heartbeats are JSONL-only.
5. The harness for execute-bead inherits zero feedback when route resolution silently fails — even when a route eventually resolves, the wedge has stolen 80 minutes of throughput.

PROPOSED FIX
A. Route-resolution timeout: the resolution step must be bounded by a configurable timeout (default 60s). On timeout: release the lease with a structured error, mark the attempt as failed, do not retry without operator attention.
B. Progress timeout independent of heartbeats: a watchdog that fires when last_activity_at has not advanced beyond a phase-specific budget (e.g., 5 minutes in 'resolving', 30 minutes in 'running' with a harness/model set, etc.) — phase-empty heartbeats with no real work are the failure signature.
C. Lease+lifecycle atomicity: claiming a bead must atomically set status to in_progress; releasing on failure must atomically clear the claim. ddx bead status counts must reflect claimed beads.
D. Operator-visible error surface: when a worker releases a lease due to timeout or wedge detection, emit a top-level operator-attention event with bead-id, attempt-id, last-activity, and a short diagnosis. ddx bead operator-attention should surface it.
E. Worker exit on repeated wedge: if the same bead wedges twice in a row, the worker should exit (or stop claiming that bead) rather than keep eating the queue.

CROSS-REF
Filed from Fizeau session 2026-05-17/18 during the claude-tui chain rollout. Adjacent bug: ddx-57c40485 (workers hold git index.lock for minutes). Both bugs share root cause: long-running operations inside the worker without bounded timeouts or operator-visible failure modes.","events_attachment":"ddx-8f2e0ebf/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-8f2e0ebf","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:workers","area:routing","kind:bug","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability"],"notes":"All 5 decomposed children landed + verified: 449baa1d (atomic Store.Release), d8970a7b (route-resolution timeout+release), dc23f001 (progress watchdog+external-close watcher), 9714eaac (consecutive-wedge claim guard), 58a69bb0 (operator-attention surface+docs). +{"closing_commit_sha":"f2d55c106c51b6b3ad47465a1778adf25ed25f57","created_at":"2026-05-11T17:54:48.993345328Z","description":"# PROBLEM
`*bead.WatcherHub` (`cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:36-66`) constructs its own `*Store` internally via `NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. This bypasses any factory-level capability validation or backend selection. Today everything is JSONL so this is invisible; once AxonStore ships and `bead.backend: axon` is configured for production, the WatcherHub still polls a local JSONL file while the authoritative bead state lives in Lakebase Postgres. Result: silent stale-data bug — `beadLifecycle` GraphQL subscriptions return empty or stale events.

# ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:54-66` — `SubscribeLifecycle` instantiates a `*Store` directly: `store := NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. No injection point for the configured backend.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:60-65` — `newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)` ties the watcher to that locally-constructed Store.
- `cli/internal/server/server.go` — constructs `*WatcherHub` and passes it to subscription resolvers. The hub is constructed once at server startup; the project Stores it creates internally are detached from any per-project factory.

# PROPOSED FIX
Change `WatcherHub` to take a `bead.BeadReader` (or `Backend`) factory function at construction:

```go
type StoreFactory func(projectID string) (bead.BeadReader, error)

type WatcherHub struct {
    factory  StoreFactory
    watchers map[string]*projectWatcher
    interval time.Duration
    ...
}

func NewWatcherHub(factory StoreFactory, interval time.Duration) *WatcherHub { ... }

func (h *WatcherHub) SubscribeLifecycle(ctx context.Context, projectID string) (<-chan LifecycleEvent, func(), error) {
    // ...
    store, err := h.factory(projectID)
    if err != nil { return nil, nil, err }
    pw = newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)
    // ...
}
```

`cli/internal/server/server.go` is updated to pass a factory that uses the project's configured backend (selected via the same config pathway that the rest of ddx-server uses).

# NON-SCOPE
- Native subscription transport on AxonStore (separate work — would replace the polling-based `projectWatcher` with native push when available).
- Changing `LifecycleEvent` shape or semantics.
- Multi-tenant identity propagation (handled separately via the `bead.WithIdentity` ctx accessor pattern).
- Modifying callers other than `cli/internal/server/server.go` and the subscription resolver.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `WatcherHub` constructor signature changes to accept a `StoreFactory` parameter. Compile-time assertion `var _ LifecycleSubscriber = (*WatcherHub)(nil)` still holds.
2. `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` no longer contains a direct `NewStore(...)` call.
3. `cli/internal/server/server.go` constructs `WatcherHub` with a factory that delegates to the same backend-selection path the rest of the server uses.
4. Test `TestWatcherHub_UsesProvidedFactory` injects a fake factory, asserts the factory is invoked when SubscribeLifecycle is called, and asserts the watcher polls the injected `bead.BeadReader` rather than the filesystem.
5. Test `TestWatcherHub_PropagatesFactoryErrors` injects a factory that returns an error and asserts SubscribeLifecycle returns the error.
6. Existing `TestWatcherHub_*` tests in `cli/internal/bead/watcher_test.go` are updated to use the factory pattern; they continue to pass.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels

A live DDx server-managed worker dogfood run allowed two workers for the same project to execute the same bead concurrently. On 2026-06-16, `ddx work status --all-projects --json` showed worker `worker-20260616T020728-afbd` (pid 1408898, attempt `20260616T021109-1940d5f0`) and worker `worker-20260616T020731-c3ac` (pid 1410320, attempt `20260616T021150-fea20ed9`) both running `ddx-6ae5134b` in separate execution worktrees. `active_work.records` only showed the second owner, so the server status surface under-reported the duplicate execution. Both workers had live Claude TUI children before the non-owner worker was stopped.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:932-972` implements `Store.ClaimWithOptions` by writing the external claim heartbeat sidecar but does not transition the tracked bead row to `in_progress`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4715-4731` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:5021-5056` rebuild candidates from `ReadyExecution`; if an `open` bead with a fresh external lease remains in the ready view, another worker can select it.
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go:578-614` starts independent external `ddx work` processes for desired workers, and there is no server-side per-project/per-bead single-flight guard before those workers enter the queue.

PROPOSED FIX
Make a fresh external claim lease remove the bead from every worker-ready path before any second worker can start an attempt. Add a regression that starts two server-managed workers against a fixture project with exactly one ready bead and proves only one attempt/worktree/provider child is created for that bead. The second worker should report a `claim_race`/idle condition or pick different ready work, never execute the same bead concurrently. Ensure `ddx work status --all-projects --json` surfaces duplicate attempt evidence if it ever detects multiple live workers with the same bead ID.

NON-SCOPE

5. review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth cap, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error transition to blocked with needs_human and do not consume no-progress budget. TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress covers this.

The execution loop now needs a single durable stop/retry policy for the reliable factory workflow. Current code still exposes older post-merge review/reopen semantics and scattered stop logic: ExecuteBeadReport.ReviewVerdict is documented as post-merge in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226, Reopen is documented for REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300, no-progress suppression only excludes a narrow transient set at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576, and C9 previously depended on ddx-cfedee8e even though C9 absorbs that scope.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1722 and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1818 contain historical cost-cap stop sites outside a first-class StopCondition gate.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300 still describe post-merge reviewer close/reopen behavior, which conflicts with ADR-024 default adversarial pre-close review.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576 counts no-progress by base/result equality but only excludes transport/quota/routing/timeout/merge_conflict outcome reasons; intake blocks, decomposition, review errors, and operator-required states must not consume no-progress retry budget.
- ddx-9228a484 previously listed ddx-cfedee8e as a dependency while also absorbing it, creating a queue deadlock.

- Implement cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go with StopCondition values Drained, Once, Signal, Budget, NoProgress, Blocked.

The pre-claim readiness payload shape lived only implicitly in the Go decoder structs and the producer prompt text, so the two drifted: the producer emitted `verdict` as a bool while the consumer parsed a string, idling a full fizeau queue (2026-05-18). Nothing in CI fails when producer and consumer disagree on the payload shape.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go` — the producer prompt text (:616-617) and the Go decoder structs (:68-119, :356-395) independently describe the same payload with no single source of truth, allowing silent drift.

PROPOSED FIX
Define the canonical payload in a shared schema file `cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json` that both the producer prompt and `decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult` reference, typing `readiness_checks[].verdict` as `oneOf [boolean, string, null]` with `additionalProperties:true` for forward-compat. Add a test that validates representative payloads (bool, string, null verdict, and a malformed kind) against the schema so producer/consumer drift fails CI.

NON-SCOPE
Does NOT implement the decoder coercion itself (decoder child). Does NOT touch the worker loop escalation or claim-rate signal, or README docs. DEPENDS ON the decoder child — the schema's verdict oneOf must match the kinds the decoder accepts. Preserves the FEAT-010 / ADR-023 readiness vocabulary.","events_attachment":"ddx-92358bd8/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-92358bd8","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:pre-claim","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability"],"parent":"ddx-f9ddaa68","priority":0,"queue-rank":950,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-7052df8b","status":"closed","title":"Lock readiness_checks JSON schema in a shared file with a CI drift gate","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:31.210909805Z"}

(Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is the umbrella command for first-time setup: installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates agent-tier links, and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.

4. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins — plugins are lazy-resolved on first use, preferring the project install over global.

5. Plugin lookup precedence at runtime:
   - <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ (or ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ in convention mode) if present.
   - ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ otherwise.
   - Hardcoded baked-in fallback (the default ddx plugin only).

6. The "ddx-validate" pre-commit hook (per ddx-777f4a09 already landed) must continue to scope its checks correctly: skill-related validation runs only when skill files are staged, regardless of install location.

7. `ddx doctor` reports both install layers: "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing" and "Project Install (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global".

SPEC IMPACT (in addition to ddx-06cbaa90's spec updates):
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-025-or-equivalent (skill install topology) — file or update.
- library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works" section — document install locations.
- library/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md (if exists) — document agent-tier paths.
- CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section — document install precedence.

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths if any exist (likely none today).
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex (add others as those harnesses define their skill paths).

When a `ddx work` orchestrator dies abnormally (SIGKILL, crash, OOM, host reboot of the supervising process) while a bead attempt is in flight, the spawned harness child (e.g. `claude --print -p --verbose --output-format stream-json ... <execute-bead>` or `codex exec --json ...`) is NOT reaped. It reparents to PID 1 and keeps running indefinitely. Observed 2026-05-27 on the dev host: a `claude --print` execute-bead child orphaned with PPID 1 for ~9h53m after its `ddx work` parent had gone — burning a harness session and (per the sibling fizeau leak) anchoring leaked subprocess trees. This is the orchestrator-side mirror of fizeau-50b6f055 (harness-runner-side leak).

ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go:156 — OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir spawns the harness via exec.Command(binary, args...) then cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd).
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:13-15 — cmdSetProcessGroup sets ONLY SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}. There is NO Pdeathsig. So the kernel does nothing automatically when the parent dies.
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:20-25 — cmdKillProcessGroup(cmd) SIGKILLs the negative pgid, but it is only invoked from the executor's graceful paths (stopProcess at executor.go:190-194, triggered by idle/wall-clock timeout, auth/rate-limit pattern, or ctx cancellation at executor.go:231-255).
- cli/cmd/root.go:49-54 — the worker context is a signal.NotifyContext on os.Interrupt + SIGTERM only. Graceful shutdown works; SIGKILL and crashes cannot be caught, so the defer/stopProcess chain never runs and the child survives.
- Net: child harness lifetime is governed entirely by parent-run cleanup. If the parent does not get to run cleanup, the child orphans. There is no kernel-enforced parent-death signal and no startup reaper that adopts/kills harness children whose parent PID is gone.

SECONDARY VECTOR
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:293 — the docker-clone backend spawns `exec.CommandContext(ctx, "docker", ...)` WITHOUT cmdSetProcessGroup, so even graceful cleanup may not reach the nested harness inside the container. (worktree/local-clone backends at attempt_backend.go:120-122 / :182-184 share the executor.go:156 path and do get Setpgid.)

PROPOSED FIX
1. Primary (Linux): set SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig = SIGKILL (or SIGTERM) in cmdSetProcessGroup so the kernel signals the harness child when the parent thread dies, regardless of how the parent died. Note the well-known caveat: Pdeathsig keys off the spawning OS *thread*, so pin/guard against Go runtime thread migration (runtime.LockOSThread around Start, or re-check parent PID in the child path). Document the macOS/Windows gap (Pdeathsig is Linux-only) and the chosen fallback there.
2. Backstop: on `ddx work` startup, reap orphaned harness children from prior dead workers — e.g. when registering/claiming, scan for execute-bead harness processes whose parent is PID 1 (or whose recorded owner PID is gone) and SIGKILL their process groups, gated to this project/workspace to avoid nuking unrelated work. Ties to the existing lease-release / operator-attention machinery (cross-ref ddx-8f2e0ebf).
3. docker-clone backend: apply cmdSetProcessGroup to the docker invocation at attempt_backend.go:293 (and/or run with --rm + a container-stop on cleanup) so the container tree is reaped too.

NON-SCOPE
- Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown already works (executor.go:231-255); do not regress it.
- The harness-runner-internal leak in fizeau is covered by fizeau-50b6f055; this bead is the ddx orchestrator -> harness-child boundary.

CROSS-REF: fizeau-50b6f055 (fizeau harness-runner process-group cleanup), ddx-8f2e0ebf (lease release / operator-attention).","events":[{"actor":"ddx bead doctor","body":"dependencies→removed dangling ddx-b06b7e85 (1 edge(s))

  During the 2026-06-11 dogfood drill, running `ddx work --once` against a dirty project root wrote `.ddx/dirty-root-guard.json`. `git status --short` then showed the guard file as untracked. A worker preflight that exits before claiming work should not leave new, unignored repository noise for the operator to triage.

ROOT CAUSE
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:958` defines the guard state filename as `dirty-root-guard.json`. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1256-1266` writes that state under the project's `.ddx` directory, and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1329-1390` updates it during dirty-root preflight. `.gitignore:420-435` ignores other DDx runtime scratch paths such as `.ddx/run-state.json`, `.ddx/run-state/`, `.ddx/workers/`, and `.ddx/*.tmp`, but does not ignore `.ddx/dirty-root-guard.json`.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Treat dirty-root guard state as DDx runtime scratch, not tracked project state.
  - Add `.ddx/dirty-root-guard.json` to the repository `.gitignore` and to the DDx init/default ignore entries in `cli/cmd/init.go` so new projects do not see the file as untracked.
  - Add or update tests in `cli/cmd/init_test.go` to prove generated gitignore content ignores `.ddx/dirty-root-guard.json` while still preserving intentionally tracked execution evidence exceptions.
  - Keep the existing dirty-root escalation behavior and state file format unchanged unless a smaller existing runtime-state helper makes the ignore behavior cleaner.

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not remove the dirty-root cooldown/escalation state machine.
  - Do not make `ddx work` proceed on a dirty project root.
  - Do not broaden ignores to all of `.ddx/`, because beads, attachments, metrics, and selected execution evidence remain intentionally tracked.

PARENT + DEPS
  No parent. No deps.","events_attachment":"ddx-94e3921b/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-94e3921b","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:git","dogfood","kind:bug","phase:iterate","preclaim"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":150,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"agent: ignore dirty-root guard state as DDx runtime scratch","updated_at":"2026-06-16T02:04:51.067345036Z"}
