2. Update parkBeadPostIntakeRejection to use the new Store.ParkToProposed (or deprecate parkBeadPostIntakeRejection entirely if it becomes redundant).
3. Replace all 6 agent call sites with the new Store.ParkToProposed method.
4. Verify no functional change: output beads must have identical status and events.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the intake rejection policy itself. Do not add new parameters or optional behavior.

- Ensure both tests assert their existing behavior (no under-specified emission; no provider spawn) while running entirely against fake harness binaries and the temp-root discovery cache.

NON-SCOPE

4. lefthook run pre-commit passes","closing_commit_sha":"fbdce1c23e9270a3c52396d08805b903d0009e0b","created_at":"2026-05-26T18:25:34.7625818Z","description":"PROBLEM
A single wedged bead can starve the queue: ddx bead ready showed 30 dispatchable beads while one single-threaded worker re-claimed and re-wedged the same bead (parent ddx-8f2e0ebf, criterion E). There is no per-bead consecutive-wedge guard — a freshly released wedged bead is immediately re-claimed and re-wedged.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: the claim/dispatch loop keeps no record of "this bead wedged on its previous claim", so it re-claims a just-released wedged bead. The only existing per-bead suppression is route-exclusion dedup at execute_bead_loop.go:4970-4978, which dedups failed (provider, model) routes — not wedge/timeout releases. A route-independent wedge (e.g. resolver hang from child ddx-d8970a7b) therefore recurs indefinitely.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Record a per-bead wedge marker when a bead is released due to route-resolution timeout (child ddx-d8970a7b) or watchdog fire (child ddx-dc23f001).
2. On the next claim cycle, if the same bead still carries a wedge marker from the immediately preceding claim (consecutive wedge), the worker does NOT re-claim it; it transitions the bead to a proposed/operator-attention state, surfaces it via an event, and continues to the next ready bead so the queue keeps draining.
3. Make the consecutive-wedge threshold configurable (default 2 consecutive wedges). Reset the marker when the bead next makes real progress (a resolved route / LLM call / commit), so a transient single wedge does not permanently sideline a bead.

NON-SCOPE
Timeout detection (child ddx-d8970a7b), watchdog (child ddx-dc23f001), operator-attention query surface + docs (child E). Reuses the release/event primitives from those children and the atomic release from child ddx-449baa1d. 6. lefthook run pre-commit passes","closing_commit_sha":"rev-ddx-977e1a7f","created_at":"2026-05-26T15:11:28.051622597Z","description":"PROBLEM
A drain worker on a full ready queue that never successfully claims (because every pre-claim attempt warns and skips) presents as healthy: loop.idle every ~50s, process alive, no error. Silent idle on a non-empty queue is a stuck state masquerading as healthy. The operator has no top-level signal that claim throughput has collapsed.

CONTEXT / WHERE
ddx work --watch is wired in cli/cmd/work.go; the drain loop is cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go. A worker-status surface already exists (cli/internal/workerstatus/ and a work_status command per cli/cmd/work_status_test.go). Claim attempts and their outcomes (claimed vs skipped/warn) pass through the loop each iteration.

PROPOSED FIX
Track claim attempt outcomes over a rolling window (default 10 attempts, configurable) in the drain loop. When the claim success rate over the window falls below a configurable threshold (default 0%, i.e. zero successes across the full window) while ready beads remain, surface a top-level operator-visible warning:
  - to stderr (a single, deduplicated warning line, not per-iteration spam), AND/OR
  - via the existing worker-status surface (cli/internal/workerstatus or ddx work status) so an operator can query it.
The window size and threshold must be configurable with documented defaults.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the decoder (ddx-606fe854), the consecutive-fingerprint escalation (ddx-d67f124e), the CI schema gate, or README docs. Only the rolling claim-success-rate signal.","events_attachment":"ddx-977e1a7f/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-977e1a7f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:workers","observability","kind:reliability","priority:reliability"],"parent":"ddx-f9ddaa68","priority":2,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"ddx work --watch: surface low claim-success-rate over a rolling window","updated_at":"2026-05-28T16:52:46.872480756Z"}

Silent idle on a full queue is a stuck state masquerading as healthy. During the Fizeau idle (2026-05-18), ddx work --watch kept polling (resource.preflight ok -> loop.idle every ~50s) while 23 beads sat ready and zero were claimed; the commit log was empty for the same 30-minute window. From the outside the worker looked alive. There was no top-level operator-visible signal that the claim success rate had collapsed to 0%.

ROOT CAUSE
The watch loop emits loop.idle at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1513 but tracks no rolling claim-success metric, so a 0% claim rate over a full queue produces the same output as a legitimately empty queue. The --watch flag is defined at cli/cmd/work.go:88.

PROPOSED FIX (parent proposed-fix E)
Track claim attempts versus successes over a configurable rolling window (default 10 attempts). When the success rate over that window falls below a configurable threshold (e.g. 0% over 10 attempts), surface an operator-visible warning via stderr and/or a status surface (ddx bead status or a ddx work status view). Add --claim-rate-window and --claim-rate-threshold flags to ddx work.

NON-SCOPE
- Decoder coercion (sibling decoder child).
- CI schema drift gate (sibling child).
- Same-fingerprint warn escalation (sibling escalation child; complementary but distinct trigger).
- README documentation (sibling child).

During the same live canary window, `.ddx/metrics/locks.jsonl` recorded an `index.lock` hold-time violation for `operation=index.commit`: duration 10027ms against the 10000ms cap. After the server restart/worker claim cycle, `.git/index.lock` remained with no live owner and blocked a tracker-only commit until manually verified with `lsof` and removed. This violates the lock lifetime contract and is a direct blocker to reliable server-managed workers.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:990-1003` documents `landEvidence` as a short ref-advancing commit boundary, then calls `CommitStaged` to create the trailing evidence commit.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1458-1460` runs `landEvidence` inside `withMainGitLock(projectRoot, "land_post_land_finalize", ...)`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:772-780` implements `CommitStaged` with a real `git commit -m ...` subprocess.
- `cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockcap.go:18-24` treats `index.lock` holds over 10s as hung and force-releases the lock. When the cap fires during the `git commit` subprocess, DDx can record the violation but still leave a stale `.git/index.lock` / interrupted commit state behind.

PROPOSED FIX
Make evidence finalization resilient to capped index commits. Either split the expensive evidence `git commit` out of the over-strict critical section or add explicit recovery/retry around cap-triggered commit interruption. The worker must remove only verified-stale `.git/index.lock` files, record durable violation evidence/operator diagnostics, and leave the landing worktree resumable so the next tracker-only or evidence commit succeeds without manual cleanup.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not raise the default 10s cap as the only fix.
- Do not remove lock metrics or suppress cap violations.
- Do not change unrelated land-coordination failure classification beyond the stale-lock recovery path.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`.
Deps: none. PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `ddx install <name>` (no --global, convention mode) installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins/ path.
- TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal asserts that when both paths have the plugin, the project copy wins.
- TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal asserts that when the project copy is missing, the global copy is used.

That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.
- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.
- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend selection config plumbing.
- Package test matrix broadening.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

Three tests reassign the package globals reapCurrentProcessProviderProbes, providerProbeCleanupFollowupDelays, providerProbeCleanupSettleQuiet/Deadline/Interval (cli/internal/server/workers_test.go:217,259,263,293,303-306) and the leaked goroutines read them concurrently -> WARNING: DATA RACE.

PROPOSED FIX
Bound the follow-up/settle cleanup goroutines to a cancelable lifetime owned by the WorkerManager (so they cannot outlive the test/manager), OR guard the mutable provider-probe globals + func hooks behind a sync.RWMutex with getter/setter helpers used by both the read sites and the three tests. Either approach must eliminate the race without changing production provider-probe reaping behavior.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change managed-worker process-tree termination (ddx-24a3da84). During dogfood of ddx-3b721804 on 2026-06-13, the worker was started with `ddx work --once --request-timeout 25m`, but the primary Claude provider process stayed alive for roughly 56 minutes while tool activity and watcher stalls continued. The operator expectation for `--request-timeout` is a bounded provider/attempt wall-clock cap with cleanup. DDx currently allowed the provider leg to run more than twice the configured timeout, which makes unattended burn-down unreliable.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:24-33 documents `DefaultProviderRequestTimeout` and says `--request-timeout` applies to execute-bead/work one-off debugging.
cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:112-125 resolves and forwards `ProviderTimeout` into the service request.
cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:337-366 resets idle/tool-call timers on service events, but there is no absolute wall-clock timer that cancels a provider session that remains active through endless tool events.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:734-787 forwards server worker request timeout into loop overrides, so server-managed workers will inherit the same ambiguity unless the boundary is made explicit and tested.

PROPOSED FIX
Make `--request-timeout` an absolute provider-session wall-clock cap for execute-bead/work attempts, distinct from idle-read and per-tool-call timers. On expiry, cancel the provider context, reap the attempt process tree, release/park the lease according to existing timeout policy, and write durable evidence naming configured_timeout, elapsed, phase, provider PID, and cleanup result.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove idle-read timeout or tool-call timeout. Do not call real providers in tests. Do not make long-running project tests impossible; if a longer cap is needed, the operator can set one explicitly.","events_attachment":"ddx-9febbad2/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-9febbad2","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:agent","area:work","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","plan:server-managed-workers"],"parent":"ddx-9d1af129","priority":0,"queue-rank":"-139","schema_version":1,"spec-id":"IP-2026-06-13-server-managed-workers","status":"closed","title":"agent: enforce request-timeout as provider wall-clock cap","updated_at":"2026-06-15T13:33:10.329745556Z"}

HELIX is now available as a marketplace plugin, so DDx should behave like an npm/npx-style plugin runner: projects record plugin intent and lock metadata, plugin payloads resolve into the shared XDG cache, and only generated agent adapter shims are materialized locally. FEAT-015 already states this forward model, and `ddx plugin install` mostly uses it through `CachePackageFromRemote` + `SyncProjectPlugin`. However, legacy registry installer code and tests still describe or exercise copying plugin payloads into `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`, which keeps the old mental model alive and makes it too easy for future changes to reintroduce checked-in marketplace assets.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/registry/installer.go:125-151` defaults a missing install root to `.ddx/plugins/<name>` and then copies the plugin tree into that project-local target. This contradicts FEAT-015's marketplace update that registry payload bytes live in `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/cache/plugins/<name>/<version>/` and project trees contain only lock metadata plus generated adapters.
- `cli/internal/registry/registry.go:73-80` and `cli/internal/registry/registry.go:91-103` still describe builtin `ddx` and `helix` registry entries in terms of `.ddx/plugins/<name>` roots and copied skill targets. Those fields may remain as manifest compatibility metadata, but the comments/default semantics should not imply registry installs copy payloads into the repository.
- `cli/internal/registry/registry_test.go:247-306` encodes the stale invariant that project-local plugin installs produce real files under `.ddx/plugins/<name>` and no symlinks. That is now backwards for marketplace installs: registry installs should produce cache-backed shims, while only `ddx plugin install --local` should create a `.ddx/plugins/<name>` symlink overlay.
- `cli/cmd/install_test.go:249-306` still names in-tree/convention install behavior around `.ddx/plugins/<name>` for local installs. The local overlay part is valid, but the test names/comments blur marketplace installs with local overlays.

PROPOSED FIX
- Split the registry package APIs explicitly: marketplace/registry installs must go through cache + lock + `SyncProjectPlugin`; legacy `InstallPackage*` copy APIs should either be renamed/marked as test-only compatibility helpers or made impossible to call from `ddx plugin install`/`ddx init` paths.
- Update builtin registry comments and manifest/default handling so `.ddx/plugins/<name>` is described only as a local overlay or legacy compatibility copy target, not the marketplace payload location.
- Replace stale tests with assertions matching the forward contract: `ddx plugin install helix` and a sample registry package write `.ddx/plugins.lock.yaml`, populate the XDG cache, materialize `.agents/skills/*` and `.claude/skills/*` as generated shims, and do not create `.ddx/plugins/<name>` payload directories. Keep separate tests proving `--local` creates a symlink overlay under `.ddx/plugins/<name>` and never updates the registry pin.
- Update docs if needed so FEAT-015, FEAT-018, SD-018, website plugin docs, and command help consistently explain the npx-style model and do not instruct users to check in marketplace payloads or generated adapter trees.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove support for local plugin overlays; `.ddx/plugins/<name>` remains valid when it is an intentional symlink to a developer checkout.
- Do not implement dependency solving or a new marketplace hosting service.
- Do not change the `SKILL.md` frontmatter contract.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; close this bead with `ddx bead close` after the implementation commit lands.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: `ddx-602ae31a` (XDG-rooted DDx state / touchless init).

During the 2026-06-13 dogfood run for ddx-d94f1857, `ddx work --request-timeout 20m` did not bound the wall-clock provider attempt. The worker exceeded the timeout, then SIGINT printed "Cancel received, shutting down gracefully" but left descendant work active long enough that the operator had to manually kill the worker/test process group.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/work.go wires request timeout into the work/try path, but the observed execution path through cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go and cli/internal/agent/service_run.go did not ensure the timeout context owned every subprocess descendant. The cleanup contract in docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md P8 requires DDx-owned execution resources and subprocesses to be reclaimed; the observed attempt violated that contract.

PROPOSED FIX
- Audit the work/try context propagation from `--request-timeout` through execute-bead and service-run so the deadline cancels the provider process group and any post-run test child.
- Add process-tree cleanup that targets only descendants owned by the attempt/worktree and records cleanup evidence.
- Make graceful shutdown wait for bounded cleanup and then hard-kill remaining attempt descendants before returning.
- Surface a durable operator-attention event if cleanup had to escalate from graceful cancellation to kill.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill unrelated interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not rely on tmux/session cleanup. After rebuilding/installing `ddx` from commit `2193aa658` and restarting `ddx-server.service` on 2026-06-16, the server came up healthy but with no child workers. Desired workers for `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx` and `/Users/erik/Projects/sesame` did not all restart until manual `ddx worker start` / `ddx worker reconcile` calls. `ddx worker start --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx` saved desired state but printed only `{"desired_count":1,...}`; a separate `ddx worker reconcile --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx` was needed before `worker-20260616T084349-8105` appeared. Server-managed workers are not reliable if boot requires an operator nudge.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/server/server.go:349-350` writes the address file and calls `reconcileDesiredWorkersAfterStartup()` before `http.ListenAndServeTLS` enters Serve.
- `cli/internal/server/server.go:364-381` performs delayed reconcile attempts in a goroutine, but the live service still had a window/state where no desired workers were running after restart.
- `cli/internal/server/server.go:397-429` discovers project roots only from `s.WorkingDir` and `s.state.GetProjects(false)` plus existing desired-state files; a missing/stale project registration or delayed state load can skip desired projects at startup.
- `cli/internal/server/worker_supervisor.go:125-137` treats absent desired-state as manage-nothing, so any skipped root produces no worker and no visible startup failure.
- `cli/cmd/worker.go:135-176` proves the manual `ddx worker start` path saves desired state then explicitly calls the reconcile endpoint, which succeeded where startup reconciliation did not.

PROPOSED FIX
Make startup reconciliation deterministic and observable. On server startup, every project with `.ddx/workers/desired.json` or a registered desired state must be reconciled after the listener is accepting requests, and failures must be surfaced in server logs/status rather than silently leaving desired workers absent. Add an integration-style regression that starts a real server instance with desired state for at least two projects, waits through startup reconciliation, and asserts workers are launched without calling the reconcile endpoint manually.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change worker claim authority or make the server own bead claims.
- Do not disable manual `ddx worker start` / `ddx worker reconcile`; they remain operator controls.
- Do not change federation worker forwarding.

Operators need to explicitly order the DDx queue. Today `ddx bead create --set queue-rank=0` stores the rank (verified by `ddx bead show`), but `ddx bead ready --json` still lists older priority-0 beads ahead of the newly ranked bead. That means a custom field is not enough in the live backend: `ddx work` can ignore the operator's intended top-of-queue item. This directly blocks forcing urgent dogfood findings, such as provider-process failure classification, to the front of the queue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `cli/internal/bead/operation.go:8-31` defines `QueueSetTop` / `QueueSetPosition` as `Extra["queue-rank"]` mutations, so the intended model is a numeric rank within the priority bucket.
- `cli/internal/bead/gaps_test.go:58-73` and `:104-120` prove JSONL store ready and execution-ready sorting honors `Extra["queue-rank"]`, but the live queue did not place `ddx-ef9df563` (priority 0, `queue-rank=0`) ahead of unranked priority-0 beads.
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:2715-2716` sorts from `beads[i].Extra["queue-rank"]`; if the active backend exposes custom fields as top-level JSON/show output but does not hydrate `Bead.Extra`, sorting silently treats ranked beads as unranked.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4997-5011` delegates worker picking to `PreviewQueue` and `ReadyExecution`, so any backend rank hydration bug affects both `ddx bead ready` and `ddx work`.

PROPOSED FIX

Make queue rank a first-class numeric field in the ready/work ordering contract across storage backends. Either hydrate `queue-rank` into `Bead.Extra` for every backend before sort, or add explicit accessors that read both Extra and backend/custom-field storage. Add command-level coverage proving `ddx bead create --set queue-rank=0`, `ddx bead update --set queue-rank=N`, and any queue helper command affect `ddx bead ready --json`, `ddx bead ready --execution --json`, `PreviewQueue`, and `ddx work` candidate selection.

NON-SCOPE

Do not replace priority buckets; rank sorts within priority unless the operator also changes priority. Do not hand-edit tracker storage. Do not rely only on JSONL tests; cover the active Axon/current backend path used by this repository.","id":"ddx-b53eb508","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:bead","area:work","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","queue-ordering"],"parent":"ddx-9d1af129","priority":0,"queue-rank":"2","queue_rank":"-10","schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"queue: honor explicit numeric rank in ready and work ordering","updated_at":"2026-06-13T21:43:34.060978688Z"}

A single attempt should not leave unrelated provider CLIs resident under the worker unless they are explicitly part of the active route and supervised. This burns resources, makes process status ambiguous, and creates exactly the cleanup risk server-managed workers must solve.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:140-142` treats Fizeau `Execute` as one event stream after process setup; DDx does not currently expose per-provider child process ownership/phase in worker status.
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:337-385` supervises service events with idle/tool timers, but it does not assert that only active-route provider children remain alive or that superseded provider processes are reaped.
- `cli/internal/agent/provider_failure.go:1-17` states untyped provider boundary failures are product bugs; the same boundary also needs process ownership evidence so stray provider CLIs do not become invisible worker state.
- Observed command: `ddx work --once --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx-provider-failure-20260613 --request-timeout 25m --route-resolution-timeout 90s`; status showed attempt `20260613T202003-18b8bd7e`, phase `running`, route logs `do route claude/sonnet`, while `ps --ppid <worker-pid>` listed Codex, Claude, and Gemini children.

PROPOSED FIX

Track provider subprocesses per attempt with route/phase metadata. When a route is selected, DDx/Fizeau should either avoid starting unrelated provider CLIs or immediately terminate any provider child not owned by the active route. Worker status/evidence should surface provider_children with provider, pid, phase, route_owner, age, and cleanup_result. At attempt end, DDx must reap every provider child and fail or flag the attempt if cleanup is incomplete.

NON-SCOPE

Do not ban legitimate short-lived helper probes if they are bounded and recorded. Do not kill unrelated same-user provider processes outside the worker process tree. The live server-managed ddx worker `worker-20260616T084349-8105` claimed `ddx-67cdc109` on 2026-06-16, ran Claude readiness intake, and then logged: `decode readiness classification result: json: cannot unmarshal array into Go struct field preClaimIntakePromptRewrite.rewrite.acceptance of type string (continuing)`. The bead remains claimed with an `intake.warn` event even though the provider route was viable and produced a syntactically useful rewrite shape. This blocks reliable `ddx work` because provider/schema drift in an optional rewrite field is treated as system-unready instead of normalized or ignored.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:47-50` defines `preClaimIntakePromptRewrite.Acceptance` as `string`, so JSON arrays for `rewrite.acceptance` fail the entire intake decode before policy can use the classification.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:255-281` already has `decodeReadinessAcceptanceList` for `suggested_child_beads[].acceptance`, tolerating either a JSON string or an array of numbered criteria.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:674-684` tells providers that suggested child acceptance may be an array but says rewrite acceptance should be `acceptance`; live Claude still returned an array for rewrite acceptance.
- `cli/internal/agent/bead_lifecycle_skill_contract_test.go:132-134` currently asserts array `rewrite.acceptance` is an error, which conflicts with live Claude TUI canary behavior and the forward-compatible worker reliability goal.

PROPOSED FIX
Normalize `rewrite.acceptance` using the same string-or-array decoder used for suggested child acceptance. Arrays of numbered criteria should be joined into the canonical multiline acceptance string before `ValidatePreClaimRewrite` runs. Malformed non-string/non-array shapes should still produce a structured decode warning/error. Update prompt/contract tests so arrays are accepted for rewrite acceptance while preserving strictness for invalid shapes.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not make arbitrary rewrite fields untyped.
- Do not accept lossy rewrites that drop or alter acceptance criteria; existing `ValidatePreClaimRewrite` must still enforce preservation.
- Do not change provider routing or model selection.

PARENT + DEPS

Deps: none. +{"acceptance":"1. The git repo health detection/fix logic currently in `cli/cmd/doctor.go` is available as a reusable helper for work-loop preflight/remediation without changing `ddx doctor --fix` behavior.
2. When a project root has `core.bare=true` but has a real working tree on disk, `ddx work` unsets `core.bare`, emits/logs the repair with issue type `git_core_bare_corruption`, rechecks git status successfully, and continues the same drain cycle.
3. When a project root has a stray local `core.worktree`, `ddx work` unsets it, emits/logs the repair with issue type `git_stray_core_worktree`, rechecks git status successfully, and continues.
4. When a project root has local `core.hooksPath`, `ddx work` unsets it, emits/logs the repair with issue type `git_local_hooks_path`, rechecks git status successfully, and continues.
5. `ddx work` does not auto-enable `extensions.worktreeConfig`; that remains warning-only unless a separate existing path explicitly enables it.
6. If known git config repair fails or git status still fails after repair, `ddx work` releases the claimed bead, emits `loop.operator_attention` with reason `pre_dispatch_git_repair_failed`, includes git stderr, and stops before claiming a second ready bead.
7. Real dirty implementation files still use the existing checkpoint-dirty behavior: `TestLoop_DrainCheckpointDirtyStopsQueue` and `TestLoop_WatchCheckpointDirtyStopsWithoutRetry` pass unchanged, including watch-mode dirty preservation behavior.
8. Add `TestWorkRepairsCoreBareAndContinuesSameCandidate` (or equivalent) proving a worker repairs `core.bare=true` and proceeds with the originally selected bead rather than failing it or moving to another bead.
9. Add `TestWorkGitRepairFailureStopsBeforeSecondClaim` (or equivalent) proving failed/unresolved repair stops after releasing the first bead and leaves the second bead unclaimed.
10. Add/extend a doctor test proving the extracted helper still powers `ddx doctor --fix` for `core.bare`, stray `core.worktree`, and local `core.hooksPath`.
11. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'CoreBare|GitRepair|CheckpointDirty|WatchCheckpointDirty|GitRepoHealth|Doctor' -count=1` passes.
12. `cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent` passes, or any pre-existing failure is documented with exact failing test names and shown unrelated to this bead.
13. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"7afa6091a8dc0496fc963f09e7c3f745dd79bf0e","created_at":"2026-06-08T04:34:08.001881019Z","description":"PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch` can burn through multiple unrelated beads and then exit with opaque operator attention when the project root's git config is corrupted, for example when `.git/config` contains `core.bare=true`. In that state `git status` fails with `fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree`, but the worker output currently degrades to `exit status 128` and repeated pre-claim warnings across distinct bead IDs. Operators should not have to stop the factory, run a separate diagnosis, and restart for a known safe DDx-era corruption mode: `ddx work` should repair the known safe git config corruptions and continue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/predispatch_dirty.go:179-183` only checks whether `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree` exits successfully. For `core.bare=true`, git can exit 0 and print `false`, so the checkpoint path proceeds as if the project root were valid instead of triggering a repair.
- `cli/internal/agent/predispatch_dirty.go:292-293` and `cli/internal/agent/predispatch_dirty.go:377-381` call git with `Output()` and wrap only `exit status 128`, dropping git stderr such as `fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree`.
- `cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go:104-106` has the same stderr-dropping pattern for readiness/lifecycle project-root status snapshots.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1153-1174` only recognizes the implementation-dirty checkpoint refusal as a project-level stop. Other pre-execute checkpoint failures fall through normal attempt failure handling, so the loop can move on to the next ready bead and eventually trip the unrelated repeated-warning operator-attention path.
- `cli/cmd/doctor.go:990-1127` already detects and fixes the known safe local git config corruptions: `core.bare=true`, stray `core.worktree`, and local `core.hooksPath`. That logic is trapped behind `ddx doctor --fix` (`cli/cmd/command_factory_commands.go:98`) instead of being available to the work-loop preflight/remediation path.
- Existing coverage around `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:916-962` proves dirty implementation files stop after one claim, but there is no equivalent test for project-root git config corruption being repaired and retried. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_checkpoint_test.go:426-430` covers ordinary dirty-file refusal, but not `core.bare=true` / non-worktree project roots.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extract/reuse the `doctor --fix` git repo health remediation as a reusable internal helper, rather than duplicating ad hoc config unsets in `ddx work`. The helper must preserve the existing safe set: unset `core.bare` when a working tree exists, unset stray `core.worktree`, unset local `core.hooksPath`; do not auto-enable `extensions.worktreeConfig` because doctor currently treats that as warning-only.
- Add a work-loop project-root git health preflight/remediator before readiness dispatch and before pre-execute checkpointing. When the known safe corruption is detected, repair it, emit durable/structured evidence naming the issue types and commands applied, then re-run the git health/status operation and continue the same drain cycle.
- Ensure a repaired first bead is not counted as a failed attempt and does not cause the worker to claim the next bead. The expected path is: detect corruption, repair, continue readiness/checkpoint for the same candidate, and then execute normally if the checkout is healthy.
- If repair fails, or if git remains unusable after the known repairs, then and only then release the claimed bead, emit `loop.operator_attention` with a stable reason such as `pre_dispatch_git_repair_failed`, include git stderr, and stop before claiming another bead.
- Preserve the existing implementation-dirty checkpoint behavior unchanged, including watch-mode preservation behavior for real dirty files.
- Add regressions for `core.bare=true` repair, failure-after-repair operator attention, and stderr preservation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not redesign execute-bead loop extraction or the unrelated `ddx-f2a366db` code-health work.
- Do not auto-repair arbitrary git repository corruption. Keep remediation limited to the known safe local config keys already handled by `ddx doctor --fix`: `core.bare`, stray `core.worktree`, and local `core.hooksPath`.
- Do not silently hide repair. The operator must see a concise log/event that DDx repaired project git config before continuing.
- Do not prove the original writer of `core.bare=true` in this bead. If the corruption source recurs after this repair path lands, file a separate prevention/investigation bead.
- Do not change Fizeau routing, provider/model selection, or readiness classification semantics except for preserving git stderr in the existing lifecycle status error.
- Do not land the existing local branch `fix/pre-dispatch-git-corruption-stop` as-is. It is a diagnostic/stop-first prototype and must be revised to repair-and-continue.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: `ddx-2a1ece2b`.

During dogfood of ddx-3b721804 on 2026-06-13, the execute-bead attempt ran a Go verification command from the wrong directory and got `go: go.mod file not found in current directory`, then later attempted to inspect `.ddx/executions/` from the wrong location and got `No such file or directory`. The same attempt temporarily moved new source files through `/tmp` while debugging. DDx instructions already say to work inside the execution worktree and keep evidence under the bundle path, but instructions alone did not prevent wrong-cwd verification or `/tmp` round-trips.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1925-1934 defines the no_changes rationale path in prose but does not make evidence-path checks mechanical for ordinary verification commands.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` explicitly proves a worker with `Reviewer: nil` can skip review and close the bead.

During the DDx dogfood run for ddx-6f25c87f, the active route was Claude/Sonnet and `ddx work status --json` reported only the Claude provider child, but the worker also spawned non-route Gemini Node wrapper processes such as `node /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/gemini`. The running provider guard did not report or reap those children, so an unavailable or misconfigured non-route provider can keep running outside the selected route. This is a core DDx/FIZ reliability failure: DDx is the surface that must keep work progressing despite provider availability and configuration variance.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/provider_children.go:68-78` classifies provider children only by `filepath.Base(parts[0])`. For Gemini installed through the Node wrapper, argv[0] is `node` and argv[1] is the `gemini` CLI path, so `providerForCommand` returns empty. Existing tests at `cli/internal/agent/provider_children_test.go:198-240` cover direct executable names (`gemini`) but not `node .../gemini`, and the process-group test at `cli/internal/agent/provider_children_test.go:242-292` uses argv[0]=gemini, so it also misses the real wrapper shape.

PROPOSED FIX
Teach provider child classification to recognize known provider CLI paths passed as wrapper script arguments, starting with Node-wrapped Gemini (`node /path/to/gemini ...`). Make the running provider guard and `ddx work status --json` treat those processes as provider=`gemini`, non-route when the active route is Claude, and reap the full process group just like direct Gemini provider children.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/provider_children.go
- cli/internal/agent/provider_children_test.go
- any existing cmd/work status test file needed to assert JSON/status behavior

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change FIZ route selection, provider/model catalogs, or provider availability probing.
- Do not add a separate supervisor or server-managed worker feature in this bead.
- Do not change the active-route preservation behavior for legitimate route-owned Claude children.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.","dogfood-source":"provider-failure-20260613","events_attachment":"ddx-cebd3b5b/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-cebd3b5b","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:agent","area:work","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","provider-supervision","dogfood"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":-680,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-7952ba18","status":"closed","title":"Detect Node-wrapped Gemini provider children in running guard","updated_at":"2026-06-14T02:19:59.975335Z"}

PROBLEM
Niflheim land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures were recorded as overall `execution_failed` even when the candidate had succeeded or landed. The worker retried implementation work instead of reconciling land/evidence state or sending the bead to operator attention, wasting route attempts and confusing system failure with model-quality failure.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative table that only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate, but execute-bead/work land outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.

PROPOSED FIX
Add self-contained Niflheim-style land coordination fixtures or table-driven cases under `cli/internal/agent/...` for `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate or merge is already landed, and for `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s` caused by generated DDx evidence. Classify these as land/ref/index/evidence coordination outcomes, not implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree is dirty with generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to existing reliability policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.

DDx work leaked a provider-specific routing requirement during dogfood: ddx work --harness codex --profile smart reached readiness with 'under-specified routing for harness="codex": supply --model, --policy, or --min-power', then continued into an execution path that launched codex exec without a model and produced no useful work. DDx/Fizeau is the surface that should handle provider availability and configuration; harness-only work requests must either resolve a viable route or fail before claim with durable actionable evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/work.go:79 defines --harness and --model as opaque passthrough constraints, and cli/cmd/work.go:113 leaves --min-power defaulting to 0. cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:244-264 builds agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest with Model, Policy, Harness, MinPower, and MaxPower, but a harness-only work request can still reach Fizeau with Model empty and MinPower 0. The comment at cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:270-272 claims Harness+empty Model routes within eligible models using Profile/MinPower, but github.com/easel/fizeau v0.14.50 rejects subprocess harness empty-model routing with no policy/min-power at service_execute.go:202-207. In dogfood this surfaced as readiness warning plus an opaque codex exec child rather than an operator-visible routing failure or automatic viable fallback.

PROPOSED FIX
Make ddx work/try execution robust for harness-only requests. Either translate profile into the Fizeau policy field that satisfies routing, apply a configured/default min-power for auto-routable harnesses, or reject before claim with a durable routing/operator-attention event when no viable route can be formed. The work loop must not continue into an execution attempt after an under-specified routing preflight for the selected harness. Add tests that simulate Fizeau's under-specified error and prove DDx chooses/fails correctly without spawning an unmodeled provider process.

NON-SCOPE
Do not hard-code Codex-only model names as the fix. Do not remove explicit model pins. Do not require every project to configure every provider manually. Do not kill unrelated interactive provider sessions.","id":"ddx-d94f1857","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:routing","area:work","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","phase:build","plan:server-managed-workers"],"parent":"ddx-9d1af129","priority":0,"queue-rank":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"20260613T130139-e3246f7f","spec-id":"IP-2026-06-13-server-managed-workers","status":"closed","title":"routing: make ddx work choose viable provider routes from harness-only requests","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:27.166615699Z"}

During the live Claude TUI canary for bead `ddx-e2fd88f3` attempt `20260616T081050-c092a258`, Claude auto-backgrounded `lefthook run pre-commit 2>&1 | tail -30` after the 120s tool timeout, then launched a second `lefthook run pre-commit 2>&1` before consuming the first background task completion. The first background task later completed exit 0; the duplicate later failed exit 1. This wastes worker time and can create false-red verification or git/index contention in a worktree that already has valid acceptance evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/harness_config.go:58-79` runs Claude through `--print -p --verbose --output-format stream-json`, which permits Claude's long-running shell commands to be auto-backgrounded by the harness surface.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2072-2078` only prohibits rerunning identical long-running commands without documenting why prior output is invalid; it does not require the agent to wait for or inspect an auto-backgrounded command's completion notification before launching the same verification command again.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go:49-94` enforces the prompt guardrails but has no `background_verification_completion` guardrail.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an explicit Claude/agent execution guardrail and regression coverage: when a long-running verification command is auto-backgrounded, the worker prompt must instruct the agent to wait for/read the background completion/output before rerunning the same command, and not start duplicate `lefthook` or `go test` processes in the same worktree unless inputs changed and the prior result is invalid. If attempt-integrity parsing already sees multiple equivalent verification commands, it should preserve the earlier successful acceptance evidence and surface the duplicate as a warning rather than converting the attempt to false-red solely because the duplicate failed.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the Claude CLI or disable Claude's backgrounding behavior.
- Do not loosen the no-red-code or staged pre-commit acceptance gates.
- Do not change routing/provider selection.

DDx decomposition guidance and implementation currently frame decomposed parents as open, execution-ineligible containers. That leaves stale open parent beads in the queue and makes the safe operational answer unclear when an agent splits a bead: the correct answer is that a losslessly decomposed bead is completed by decomposition and should be closed. The downstream invariant is that children must still be work-ready: `parent` is hierarchy metadata, not a dependency gate, and generated children must not depend on the decomposed parent.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/triage/decomposition.md:20-22 says `too_large_decomposed` leaves the parent `status=open` with dependency edges to children, and docs/triage/decomposition.md:63-73 says the parent remains open and depends on all children.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:162,169,171,180,197-207 says review/no_changes/decompose outcomes leave oversized or decomposed beads open with child dep edges or `execution-eligible=false`.
- .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:210-213 and .claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:210-213 forbid `ddx bead close <bead-id>` for the current bead and only allow a parent-note update during Step 0 decomposition, so agent-facing instructions do not say to mark a decomposed bead completed.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2734-2743 logs successful pre-claim decomposition then releases the parent while explicitly commenting that it stays open until children close. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:5248-5275 implements `applyPreClaimDecomposition` by setting `execution-eligible=false` instead of closing the parent.
- cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:130-133 and 207-210, plus cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go:182-204, use the same park-open pattern.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_decompose_test.go:73-77 and cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose_test.go:20-22 assert the old open/ineligible behavior, so tests lock in the ambiguous framing.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the decomposition lifecycle contract in docs/triage/decomposition.md, docs/beads.md, TD-031, SD-025, ADR-023, ADR-024, and FEAT-010 where applicable: a lossless child/sibling/replacement decomposition closes the decomposed bead as completed-by-decomposition. Historical open `execution-eligible=false` containers may remain as legacy/backfill cases, but they are not the normative path for new decomposition.
- Update .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md, .claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md, cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go, and prompt snapshots so Step 0 says: agents may create child/sibling/replacement beads, wire only child-to-child or sibling/replacement deps, must not add the parent as a child dependency, and must close the decomposed parent once the split is lossless and durable.
- Update `applyPreClaimDecomposition`, `runDecomposer`, and `AutoDecomposeEpic` to close the parent with a lifecycle transition after child creation. Preserve traceability via `triage-decomposed` / `decompose-applied` events and child IDs in Extra or event body. Clear claim metadata/heartbeat so a closed parent cannot remain half-owned.
- Preserve downstream readiness semantics: children are created with `Parent=<decomposed-id>` but no dependency on the parent. Existing child-to-child dependency ordering remains allowed. Closing the parent must not be used as a substitute for adding or removing legitimate child-to-child deps.
- Update and add tests around the changed lifecycle behavior and the child readiness invariant.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the broader `parent_ancestor_in_deps` doctor/back-edge repair slice tracked by ddx-130cf9ab. This bead must not regress it and should reuse its invariant, but it does not replace that repair/backfill work.
- Do not redesign epic auto-close or historical `dead_intermediate_close`; keep those as legacy/backfill behavior for already-open containers.
- Do not drain or close existing decomposed queue entries as part of this bead. File or run a separate reconciliation bead if historical tracker cleanup is needed.
- Do not change Fizeau routing, model escalation, or provider selection.

INTERSECTIONS
- Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b (ddx work idle-path diagnose and auto-remediate). This bead clarifies one remediation path so the queue advances instead of preserving dead intermediates.

The desired operator model is npx-like: a project records plugin intent/version pins, plugin payloads resolve into an XDG cache or compatible global install, and local agent skill directories are generated shims/links that can be recreated at any time.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-015-installation-architecture.md:60 still described `ddx init` as writing `.agents/skills/` and `.claude/skills/` project files.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-015-installation-architecture.md:63 described registry plugin install as writing real files under `<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-015-installation-architecture.md:317 said `ddx init` and registry plugin installs copy real files for clone portability.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md:46 described default project install as plugin content under project/convention plugin trees with agent-tier outputs under the project.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-018-plugin-api.md:58 described `install.root` as copying the entire plugin to a target and had no materialization/cache contract.
- cli/cmd/install.go:150 calls `adjustInstallTargets` and `registry.InstallPackage`, which copies package payloads into the resolved install root.
- cli/cmd/install.go:216 rewrites package install targets to `plugins/<name>`, preserving the copy-tree model for registry installs.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement the marketplace materialization contract now documented in FEAT-015, FEAT-018, and ADR-027:
1. Add project plugin dependency/lock metadata for registry installs. The project records plugin name/version/source/checksum or equivalent durable lock data; the full plugin payload is not committed into the project worktree.
2. Resolve registry plugin payloads into an XDG cache/global package layer, with the baked-in ddx package as the offline fallback for the default ddx plugin.
3. Add `ddx plugin sync` or equivalent lazy sync path that materializes `.agents/skills/*` and `.claude/skills/*` as generated shims/links from the lock/cache, and make first plugin-consuming commands call it when shims are missing.
4. Update `ddx plugin install`, `ddx plugin list/show`, `ddx doctor --plugins`, and uninstall/upgrade behavior to distinguish project dependency metadata, cached payloads, generated shims, local overlays, and legacy copied payloads.
5. Preserve `ddx plugin install <name> --local <path>` as a developer overlay that may symlink directly to a checkout.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/cmd/install.go and install tests in cli/cmd/install_manifest_test.go or sibling tests.
- cli/internal/registry/* for package resolution, cache paths, lock metadata, and validation.
- cli/cmd/doctor*.go and doctor plugin tests for reporting cache/materialization state.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-015-installation-architecture.md, docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-018-plugin-api.md, docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md for any implementation-driven refinements.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove local overlay support.
- Do not make HELIX a built-in DDx payload.
- Do not require project repos to commit marketplace plugin payload trees.

Underspecified worker/provider-process failures are a DDx correctness bug. DDx/FIZ is the abstraction that should keep work moving across provider availability and local configuration variance. If a provider executable is missing, a harness process exits early, model routing fails, or Fizeau returns a pre-dispatch/provider-boundary error, `ddx work` must classify it with a typed provider/process reason, record fallback/stop evidence, clean up any children, and continue when an alternate route is eligible. It must not surface a generic worker failure, raw "routing under-specified" text, or leave the bead/operator unable to tell whether this is provider_auth, provider_model_unavailable, provider_harness_unavailable, provider_connectivity, no_viable_provider, provider_config_invalid, or unknown_provider_failure.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `cli/internal/agent/provider_failure.go:1-17` defines the intended DDx/Fizeau boundary contract: generic untyped provider failures are product bugs, and each provider failure needs typed reason, retryability, fallback decision, and durable evidence.
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:140-142` still returns `fmt.Errorf("agent: execute: %w", err)` for Fizeau `Execute` setup failures before the event stream exists; every caller must prove this error is classified and does not fall through as generic execution/worker failure.
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:337-385` bounds event-stream silence/tool loops, but provider-process startup/early-exit failures can occur before useful stream evidence, so they need a separate typed classification/cleanup path.
- `cli/cmd/zero_config_work_test.go:15-27` documents the prior "routing under-specified" regression and explicitly avoids real provider dispatch; coverage must be extended from "do not print under-specified" to "classify provider-process failure and choose fallback/stop semantics."
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_escalation_aborted_test.go:18-60` and `:285-342` prove provider_connectivity fallback semantics after reports exist, but do not prove raw provider-process failures are converted into those reports before worker/operator surfaces see them.

PROPOSED FIX

Add an end-to-end worker/process-boundary classification path for provider process startup, missing executable, early child exit, model-unavailable, under-specified routing, and Fizeau pre-dispatch errors. Normalize them through the provider failure taxonomy, write `ProviderFailureEvidence`, include requested/resolved harness/provider/model plus retryability and fallback_stop_reason, and ensure unpinned workers automatically retry eligible alternate routes while pinned workers stop loudly with hard_pin_exhausted. Ensure cleanup reaps provider-process children before the worker continues.

NON-SCOPE

Do not move model catalog/routing ownership out of Fizeau. Do not call real Claude/Codex/Gemini providers in tests. Do not silently widen explicit operator pins. Do not weaken existing provider_connectivity/no_viable_provider fallback tests.","events_attachment":"ddx-ef9df563/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-ef9df563","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:agent","area:work","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","provider-failure"],"parent":"ddx-9d1af129","priority":0,"queue-rank":-300,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-b9914fff","status":"closed","title":"agent: type worker provider-process failures before generic failure","updated_at":"2026-06-13T22:41:22.739101839Z"}

6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-06-13T13:55:11.417260583Z","description":"PROBLEM
In a linked DDx worktree, `ddx bead show/export` reported ddx-d94f1857 as closed while `ddx work plan --json` still ranked the same bead as open and next claim. That can make a worker re-run already-completed work and breaks the queue as source of truth.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/bead.go:286-320 resolves bead commands through beadWorkspaceRoot(), which calls git.FindNearestDDxWorkspace and can prefer a registered/master worktree. cli/cmd/work_plan.go:80-84 bypasses that path and opens bead.NewStore(resolveBeadStoreRoot(projectRoot)), which prefers the local in-tree .ddx if present. In dogfood on 2026-06-13, the global worktree registry master pointed at an old execute-bead temp path, so bead commands and work plan observed different physical stores.

PROPOSED FIX
- Centralize queue store resolution for all bead/work/status commands behind one project-aware helper.
- Add a linked-worktree regression fixture where the local .ddx snapshot disagrees with the registered/master store and prove bead show, bead list, bead export, bead ready, work plan, and work status use the same store.
- Add a diagnostic when the worktree registry master points at a missing execute-bead path or a path outside the current repository family.
- Ensure the chosen store is visible in verbose/debug output so operators can identify split-brain state.

NON-SCOPE
Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl as the fix. Do not remove convention-root support.
